misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's handling of fees and potential discounts could mislead the student regarding the financial implications of applying to IILM Greater Noida.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko 100% ki scholarship mil jaati hai instant first year ke liye.
incidents.translation: You get a 100% scholarship instantly for the first year.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims a 100% scholarship is guaranteed without clarifying the conditions or merit requirements, which can mislead the student into thinking it's easily attainable.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar main aapko bata deti hoon ki aapka NIET ka interview round hota hai uske baad aapka selection hota hai.
incidents.translation: If I tell you that there is an interview round for NIET, after that your selection happens.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that admission is almost guaranteed after an interview, which can mislead the student about the competitiveness of the admission process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi iske paas bhi slots kafi kam hain.
incidents.translation: Currently, there are very few slots available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting limited availability of slots without providing evidence, which can pressure the student to make a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several misleading claims regarding scholarships, admission processes, and urgency that could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar abhi aapko free ka karna hai to Amity ka kar sakte ho, theek hai? Amity ka aapka free of cost ho jayega Gurgaon ka aur other reason mein bhi college iske campus hai.
incidents.translation: If you want to do it for free now, you can do Amity, okay? Your Amity will be free of cost in Gurgaon and there are other campuses as well.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the application to Amity as free of cost without clarifying any conditions or potential fees, which could mislead the student into thinking there are no costs involved.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi nahi pura ready hai wo to classes start ho gayi hain last year bhi wahan pe kaafi admission hue hain.
incidents.translation: No, no, it is fully ready, classes have started there, many admissions happened last year.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor assures the student that the college is fully operational and ready for classes, which could be misleading if the college is not actually prepared or if there are issues with the program.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding the free application process and the readiness of the college, which could influence the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi aapka process ho gaya na to abhi selection ho jayega, baad me to rejection ka koi dikkat hi nahi aayega.
incidents.translation: Since your process is done now, you will get selected now, later there won't be any issue of rejection.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that completing the application process guarantees admission, which is misleading and creates unnecessary pressure.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To inke paas kaafi ye jo May ka time hai kaafi admissions aate hain. To is wajah se aapka sabse zyada rejection isi time pe hota hai.
incidents.translation: During May, they receive a lot of admissions. That's why most rejections happen during this time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying early guarantees a higher chance of acceptance, which is a false claim and manipulative.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the admission process and created a sense of urgency that could pressure the student into applying without proper consideration.

incidents.college_or_course: UEM, Chandigarh University, Amity University Jaipur
incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekhiye agar aap karna chahte hain to Chandigarh location sahi rahega aapke liye? ... kyunki free hai, kuch paise isme ja nahi rahe hain
incidents.translation: If you want to do it, then Chandigarh location will be good for you? ... because it's free, no money is being spent on this
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the cost is the only consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi kuch time ke liye free kari hai limited kyunki abhi 30 tarikh tak to waise hi close ho rahe hain saare
incidents.translation: currently, they have made it free for a limited time because all will close by the 30th
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the application will close soon, which may not be true, thus pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the free application as a primary motivator and created a false sense of urgency, which can mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap abhi shaam tak, matlab abhi 3:00 hi baj rahe hain, shaam abhi hone wali hai, kyunki 6:00 baje tak hum bhi nikal jaayenge. To aap abhi.
incidents.translation: So you should complete it by this evening, it's only 3:00 PM now, evening is coming, because we will leave by 6:00 PM. So you should do it now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the student must complete the application before a specific time, which may not be necessary or true.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur vo aapko unhone bas aapko thoda sa laalach yeh diya hai ki examination Kolkata mein nahi hoga, aap se application karoge to seat fix ho jaayegi.
incidents.translation: And they have just given you a little temptation that the examination will not be in Kolkata, if you apply, your seat will be fixed.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying will guarantee a fixed seat, which is misleading as it implies a certainty that is not typically the case in college admissions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed misleading urgency and overpromised admission outcomes, which could mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To ye cheez hai ki jahan pe charges nahi hai to at least wo to hum option leke chale na aur Amity ka bhi average package 8-10 lakh ka hota hai aapko pata hoga kahin na kahin to, globally hai Amity, pan India me hai plus out of India bhi hai.
incidents.translation: So this is the thing that where there are no charges, at least we should take that as an option, and Amity also has an average package of 8-10 lakh, you must know that somewhere, it is global, it is in pan India and also out of India.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying to Amity University is a no-cost option and emphasizes its global presence and average package without providing clear evidence or context, which can mislead the student into thinking it's a guaranteed opportunity.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To wo saari cheezein aapke liye kahin na kahin ek beneficial part hai aur aage aap jab apply karoge na to aapko cheezein better hi milengi usme kahin na kahin, ye cheez aapko pata hi hai, hai na?
incidents.translation: So all these things are somewhere a beneficial part for you, and when you apply later, you will get better things in it, you know this, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying will automatically lead to better opportunities without acknowledging the competitive nature of admissions, which can mislead the student into thinking that applying guarantees better outcomes.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about the benefits of applying to Amity University that could mislead the student regarding the urgency and guaranteed outcomes of the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Unhone mere se bola tha ki aap form bharwa do. Ke aapko achha rahega.
incidents.translation: They told me to fill out the form. That it would be good for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that filling out the form guarantees a positive outcome, which can mislead the student into thinking admission is assured.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To unhone mere se bola tha ki aaj shaam tak ya aaj usse pehle pehle form bharwa dena 1500 rupaye unhone mere ko fees batayi thi.
incidents.translation: They told me to fill out the form by tonight or before today, and they told me the application fee is 1500 rupees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting a deadline for form submission without clear justification, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the admission process and created a false sense of urgency regarding application deadlines.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapko better lag raha hai to dekho aapka interest bahut zyada zaroori hai kyunki padhai aapko karni hai, correct? Doosri cheez mann mein yeh shanka nahi honi chahiye ki yaar mere paas wo college bhi tha, maine usme apply nahi kiya us time pe.
incidents.translation: If you feel better about it, then see, your interest is very important because you have to study, right? The second thing is that there should be no doubt in your mind that I had that college too, and I didn't apply to it at that time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by suggesting that the student might regret not applying to a college, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aise nahi bharti hai na, branch rehti hai seats, main wahi to keh raha hoon. Bahut rare hota hai wo cheez.
incidents.translation: That's not how admissions work; branches have seats, that's what I'm saying. It's very rare that this happens.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor downplays the urgency created by the college by stating that seats are usually available, which contradicts the earlier pressure to apply quickly, creating confusion.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed pressure tactics that could mislead the student into making a rushed decision regarding college applications, particularly emphasizing potential regret and urgency without clear justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: The student will get a 100% placement guarantee.
incidents.translation: The student will get a 100% placement guarantee.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim of a '100% placement guarantee' is misleading as it implies a certainty of job placement that cannot be assured, which is a clear overpromise.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a misleading claim regarding placement guarantees, which could unduly influence the student's decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan IILM me aapko easily aapko admission aapko mil jayenge.
incidents.translation: Yes, you will easily get admission in IILM.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's statement suggests a guarantee of admission without acknowledging the student's actual qualifications or the competitive nature of the admissions process, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: And uske basis pe aapko scholarship bhi aapko mil jayenge ye.
incidents.translation: And based on that, you will also get a scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student will definitely receive a scholarship based on their JEE score without clarifying that it is contingent on other factors, which can mislead the student into thinking it is guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Because in case abhi to teen chaar din pehle to abhi available hua tha ispe 80% off tha.
incidents.translation: Because just three to four days ago, there was an 80% off on this.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting a limited-time offer without providing evidence of the claim, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several misleading claims regarding admission guarantees and scholarship availability, as well as creating a false sense of urgency, which could pressure the student into applying without adequate consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab ye to aapka ho hi gaya matlab option. Ab waise isme aap decide kar lena matlab ghar pe baat kar sakte ho aap.
incidents.translation: Now this has become your option. Now you can decide, you can talk at home.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dusra usme center wagera choose hota hai to center wagera agar aap choose karoge to fir wahan pe hame exam dene ke liye usme jana padta hai.
incidents.translation: Secondly, if you choose a center, then you have to go there to give the exam.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting the need to choose a center for an exam, which may not be necessary, creating a false sense of urgency.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application process and the urgency regarding exam centers indicates potential misselling practices.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity Mumbai ka form fill kyun nahi kiya dear, woh to aapka abhi right now aapka free of cost aapka form bhara jaata hai, uska fill kyun nahi kiya aapne form, mujhe batao.
incidents.translation: Why haven't you filled the form for Amity Mumbai, dear? It's currently free of cost to fill the form, why haven't you done it?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing that the application is free as a primary reason to encourage the student to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To bacche kya karte hain pehle hi directly basically apply kar dete hain, by chance hamara aapka cut off zyada jaati hai to bacche pehle hi apply kar deta hai.
incidents.translation: So what students do is they apply directly beforehand, just in case our cutoff goes higher, students apply early.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying early guarantees a better chance of admission, which can mislead the student into thinking that early application is a sure way to secure a seat.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included pressure to apply based on the free application and implied guarantees regarding admission chances, which are misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh yeh hai ki agar aap exam doge toh scholarship ke chances hain na yahan pe.
incidents.translation: So this is that if you give the exam, then there are chances of scholarship here.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that there are guaranteed chances of scholarships based on the exam, which can mislead the student into thinking they will receive financial aid without clear conditions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh fir agar aapko uss tarike se mil raha hai jahan pe aap apna woh dikha sakte ho toh achha hoga aapke liye.
incidents.translation: So if you are getting it in such a way where you can show your capabilities, then it will be good for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that admission is highly likely if the student performs well in the exam, which can create an unrealistic expectation of guaranteed admission based on performance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarship opportunities and admission likelihood that could mislead the student regarding the actual conditions and chances of success.

incidents.counsellor_quote: May 15 tak abhi woh bata rahe hain May 15 tak abhi woh open rahega woh.
incidents.translation: They are saying it will remain open until May 15.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a specific deadline for applications that may not be substantiated, creating a sense of urgency without clear evidence.

incidents.counsellor_quote: JIMS mein jahan tak chances hai ki JIMS aapko ho jayega. Mil jayega aapko JIMS.
incidents.translation: As far as chances go, you will get admission in JIMS.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests a high likelihood of admission to JIMS without providing any concrete basis for this assurance, which can mislead the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed signs of misselling by creating a false sense of urgency regarding application deadlines and overpromising admission chances to JIMS.

incidents.counsellor_quote: try karo ki aaj hi aap apply kar lo because 30th na, aaj iska last date hai for the applications.
incidents.translation: Try to apply today because the last date for the applications is today, the 30th.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for applications without providing evidence or confirmation of this deadline, which may not be accurate.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the application deadline creates undue pressure on the student to act quickly without verifying the claim.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ye aapka fees ke liye hoga Bharti Vidyapeeth ka. Iska aapka aapka form ka charge lagega iska aapka Bharti Vidyapeeth ka.
incidents.translation: This will be for your fees for Bharti Vidyapeeth. There will be a charge for your form for Bharti Vidyapeeth.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor initially implied that applying to certain campuses was free, but later revealed that there is a fee for the Bharti Vidyapeeth application, which could mislead the student about the costs involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor misled the student regarding the application fees for Bharti Vidyapeeth, which constitutes a clear case of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko khaali abhi apply karte usme scholarship bhi milti hai, theek hai? Exam wagera ke base pe, interview ke base pe, usme aapko clearly mention hai scholarship ka hai usme.
incidents.translation: You should just apply now, you will also get a scholarship in it, okay? Based on the exam, based on the interview, it is clearly mentioned that there is a scholarship in it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes applying for Plaksha University primarily based on the free application and potential scholarships without adequately discussing the actual fit or merits of the college, which can mislead the student into thinking it's a guaranteed opportunity.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar scholarship mili to aap opt kar sakte ho, main ye nahi keh raha hoon ki aapko karna hi karna hai.
incidents.translation: If you get a scholarship, then you can opt for it, I am not saying that you have to do it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student can opt for the college if they receive a scholarship, implying that the scholarship is a likely outcome, which can create a false sense of security about the financial aspect of attending the college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on free applications and potential scholarships for Plaksha University without discussing the college's actual fit raises concerns about misrepresentation and pressure tactics.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Mumbai ka free chal raha hai to wo bhi fill kar le.
incidents.translation: There is a free application going on for Mumbai, so he should fill it too.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free application as a primary reason for the student’s friend to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application creates a misleading incentive for the student and their friend to apply without considering other important factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: KCC mein almost seats jo hain wo fill ho chuki hain, ab bas mujhe lagta hai do ya chaar din mein wo CSE ke liye lena band kar denge.
incidents.translation: Almost all the seats in KCC are filled, I think they will stop taking admissions for CSE in the next two to four days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by claiming that seats are almost full and admissions will soon close, without providing evidence to support this claim.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap agar convert nahi karoge, aap mujhse connect nahi karoge to main, main bhool jaati hoon ki mujhe kis kis bachche ko connect karna hai.
incidents.translation: If you do not convert, if you do not connect with me, then I forget which students I need to connect with.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement implies that the student must act quickly to secure their admission, suggesting a false sense of control over the admission process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor applied pressure by creating a false sense of urgency regarding seat availability and implied that the student needed to act quickly to secure admission, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To usme aap befikar raho wo aate hain companies aur inka placement bhi aap dekhoge agar achha hai.
incidents.translation: So you don't have to worry, companies come to universities, and you will see that their placement is good.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that companies will definitely come for placements at CGC Mohali, which may not be guaranteed and can mislead the student about the actual placement scenario.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Highest to 35 ke aas paas gaya tha aur jo average inka rehta hai average.
incidents.translation: The highest went around 35, and the average remains average.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents placement figures in a vague manner, suggesting high numbers without clear context or verification, which can mislead the student about the college's actual placement performance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about placements that could mislead the student regarding the actual opportunities at CGC Mohali, creating an impression of guaranteed success.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan only South India then you can apply for Amity Bangalore, if you will say me like for today they have free of cost application also.
incidents.translation: Yes, only South India then you can apply for Amity Bangalore, if you tell me that for today they have free of cost application as well.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits, which can mislead the student into thinking the application is the most important factor.

incidents.counsellor_quote: So if you want to apply because you know that JEE exam is also out, like result is out na, so most of the good colleges are closing now their application.
incidents.translation: So if you want to apply because you know that the JEE exam is also out, like the result is out, so most of the good colleges are closing their applications now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that many colleges are closing applications soon, which may not be accurate or substantiated, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency and the appeal of a free application to push the student towards applying, which could mislead the student about the importance of the decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To abhi aapke paas jo options bache hue hain na to make sure ki aap jitni jaldi aap form apply kar lo utni jaldi aapke liye beneficial rahega.
incidents.translation: So right now, the options you have left, make sure that the sooner you apply for the form, the more beneficial it will be for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that options are limited and that applying quickly is crucial, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision without considering all options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: JIMS bhi close hone wala hai, Fostima bhi aapka close hone wala hai, FIIB bhi close hone wala hai. Jo bhi top most universities hain lagbhag lagbhag close ho rahi hain.
incidents.translation: JIMS is also about to close, Fostima is also about to close, FIIB is also about to close. Almost all top universities are closing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that multiple colleges are about to close their admissions, which may not be true and pressures the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Easily ho jayega.
incidents.translation: It will happen easily.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor assures the student that admission will be easy based on their percentile, which can be misleading as admission processes can vary and are not guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics and inflated urgency regarding college admissions, which could mislead the student into making a rushed decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap multiple colleges dekhoge, multiple colleges mein baat karoge, fir comparison karoge, fir fees ka dekhoge, kahan fees achhi hai, kahan scholarship achha hai, kahan pe mujhe placements achhe milenge.
incidents.translation: You will look at multiple colleges, talk to multiple colleges, then compare, then see the fees, where the fees are good, where the scholarship is good, where I will get good placements.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student will easily find good placements and scholarships across multiple colleges without acknowledging the competitive nature of admissions and the variability in outcomes, which can mislead the student into thinking success is guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: LPU mein kya hai wo apna own entrance exam karate hain. To jab entrance exam hota hai to uske basis pe na student ko scholarship milta hai 30 to 70% ka.
incidents.translation: LPU has its own entrance exam. When the entrance exam happens, based on that, students get scholarships ranging from 30 to 70%.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the scholarship as a guaranteed benefit of taking the entrance exam without clarifying that it is contingent on performance, which can mislead the student into thinking they will receive a scholarship regardless of their exam results.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about placements and scholarships that could mislead the student regarding the certainty of outcomes, indicating a potential for misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi kya hai ki isme hai coupon code college ka, jaise application fees hoti hai na har ek college ki, kisi ki 1000 hoti hai, kisi ki 2000 hoti hai, kisi ki 1500 hoti hai, pata hoga aapko. To isme abhi coupon code available hai aaj ke liye, to agar aap aaj mein apply karte ho to aapko main coupon code dunga to usse kya hoga ki jo aapka 1000 rupaye ka form hai wo aapka 500 rupaye mein hi complete ho jayega.
incidents.translation: Right now, there is a coupon code for the college, as you know, application fees vary for each college, some are 1000, some are 2000, some are 1500. So currently, there is a coupon code available for today, so if you apply today, I will give you a coupon code that will reduce your 1000 rupee form to just 500 rupees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the coupon code is only available for today, which may pressure the student into making a quick decision without considering other options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to abhi kya hai ki result bhi aapka aaya hua hai to abhi benefit aapko ye milega ki jaise abhi agar aap apply karte ho to Amity ka waise form hota hai 1500 rupaye ka, shayad pata hoga aapko, correct? But abhi jaise kuch kuch campus mein na Amity mein koi charges nahi hai, free of cost application ho raha hai aapka.
incidents.translation: So now that your result has come out, you will benefit from applying now. The Amity form usually costs 1500 rupees, as you might know. But currently, for some campuses, the application is free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the application is free for certain campuses without providing clear evidence or details, which may mislead the student into thinking they have a guaranteed opportunity without costs.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that created a sense of urgency and made claims about free applications that could mislead the student regarding the actual costs and application processes.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi abhi nahi lag raha bas ek ya do din tak hi hai wo might be shayad aaj bhi close ho sakta hai koi surety nahi hai to be very honest.
incidents.translation: Right now there is no charge, it might be free for just a day or two, it could close today, there's no surety to be very honest.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the application might close soon without any evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: NIET ka to abhi kuch slots pe jo application chal raha hai na aapka free chal raha hai usme aisa koi certain koi amount pay nahi karna pad raha hai abhi ke liye bas kuch hi kuch hi dino ke liye bas.
incidents.translation: Currently, the application for NIET is free for a few slots, and you don't have to pay any amount for now, just for a few days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the application is free for a limited time, which can mislead the student into thinking they have a guaranteed opportunity without clarifying the actual terms.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics and created a sense of urgency regarding application deadlines, which could mislead the student into making quick decisions without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan ji, abhi seats available hain but jaldi fill ho rahi hain, to aap jaldi se process complete kar lijiye.
incidents.translation: Yes, seats are available right now but they are filling up quickly, so please complete the process soon.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that seats are filling up quickly without providing evidence or context, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the urgency of seat availability is misleading and could pressure the student into applying without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity me Gurgaon me free hai application.
incidents.translation: The application is free at Amity in Gurgaon.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that this is the most important factor rather than the college's fit for their needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: waha se bhi there is a chances aapko ho jayega waha pe.
incidents.translation: There is a chance you will get admission there.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student is likely to get admission without providing concrete criteria or conditions, which can create a false sense of security about the admission process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about free applications and the likelihood of admission that could mislead the student regarding the importance of these factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho isme seat na agar aap check karoge to mushkil se jo seat hogi na aapki 60-70 ke near aapki seat hogi.
incidents.translation: Look, if you check the seats, there will be hardly 60-70 seats available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is fabricating a sense of urgency by claiming a limited number of seats without evidence, pressuring the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: ab yahan pe kya hai jab aapki counselling hogi na to ek real based wo aapko amount bata sakenge.
incidents.translation: Now what happens here is that when your counselling happens, they will tell you the actual amount.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student will definitely get a clear understanding of costs and admission outcomes during counselling, which may not be guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise clarity on admission outcomes, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: NIET ka aap, haan aaj karte ho to free hai NIET ka.
incidents.translation: If you apply today, the NIET application is free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free only for today, which creates a sense of urgency that may not be warranted, pressuring the student to act quickly without considering other options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka jo abhi application beta chal raha hai na wo free of cost chal raha hai.
incidents.translation: The Amity application is currently free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that the application is free at this moment, which may not be true or may be misleading, creating a false sense of urgency for the student to apply immediately.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency tactics regarding free applications for NIET and Amity, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap first come first serve ke base pe abhi apply nahi karoge toh to be very frank hamare paas 100 me se jo 100 students aa rahe hain, woh matlab 100 me se jo 97 students aa rahe hain, woh B.Tech CSE branch ke liye aa rahe hain.
incidents.translation: If you do not apply on a first come first serve basis right now, to be very frank, out of 100 students, 97 students are coming for the B.Tech CSE branch.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the majority of students are applying for the same course, implying that the student may miss out on admission if they delay, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh aapko in colleges me milna bahut mushkil hai. Ab aapko do se teen college me apply karna padega, unke entrance exams vagera dene padenge, tab jaake aapko kisi ek college me admission mil payega.
incidents.translation: So it will be very difficult for you to get into these colleges. Now you will have to apply to two to three colleges, give their entrance exams, and only then will you be able to get admission in one college.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that admission is guaranteed only if the student applies to multiple colleges and takes entrance exams, which can create undue pressure and mislead the student about their chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used manipulative language to create urgency and overpromised admission outcomes, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Sirf free ke application daal ke aap wait nahi kar sakte na ki aapko usse admission mil jayega.
incidents.translation: You can't just submit free applications and wait for admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that simply applying for free won't lead to admission, which pressures the student to consider paid applications without providing sufficient justification for why they should choose KCC specifically.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapke is saal jo aapne 12th ka paper diya hai agar usme aapke 75% plus PCM mein aa jaate hain to wo log aapko 25% tak ki scholarship provide kar denge.
incidents.translation: If you score 75% or more in PCM in your 12th this year, they will provide you with a scholarship of up to 25%.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the scholarship as a guaranteed outcome based on achieving a specific score, which may not be a definite promise from the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap wait nahi kar sakte in cheezon ke liye.
incidents.translation: You cannot wait for these things.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency that may not be warranted, pressuring the student to make a decision quickly without fully exploring their options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited multiple instances of misselling by creating urgency and overpromising outcomes related to admissions and scholarships, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Chances hain, 80% chances hain ki aapko mil jayenge.
incidents.translation: There are chances, there are 80% chances that you will get it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting an 80% chance of admission without any basis or evidence, which can mislead the student into thinking their admission is almost guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap try to karo. Kal se aapne kitne, aapke pehle kitne baccho ne fill kar diya hoga.
incidents.translation: You should try. How many of your peers have filled it out by now?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that many others are applying, which may pressure the student to act quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about admission chances and created urgency without sufficient justification, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan application form aapka free hai, baaki interview wagera aap dete ho, interview ke baad jo aapka selection hota hai na 30,000 fir aapko registration fee pay karni hoti hai, woh aapka, woh aapko pay karni padti hai, usme hum koi discount nahi hota but aapko application form mein main discount kara dungi to usme aapko koi costing nahi lagegi, woh free ho jayega bilkul, bilkul aapka.
incidents.translation: Yes, your application form is free, but you have to pay a registration fee of 30,000 after the interview selection. There is no discount on that, but I will arrange a discount for the application form so that it will be completely free for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is misleading the student by suggesting that the application form is free while downplaying the subsequent registration fee, which is a significant cost. This creates a false impression of no financial commitment.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But agar aap wohi Varanasi, Allahabad wagera jaoge na wahan pe aapko placements ki guarantee nahi milti kyunki woh andar ki taraf hai location, wahan pe itni companies aati bhi nahi hai.
incidents.translation: But if you go to Varanasi or Allahabad, you won't get a guarantee of placements because those locations are inward and not many companies come there.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that certain locations will not provide placement opportunities without providing evidence or context, which could unduly influence the student's decision-making.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the application process and the guarantees of placements based on location, which could pressure the student into making a decision without full transparency.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan ye in colleges ki na saare almost jitne bhi colleges main bata rahi hu na, in sabki almost last date aapki yahi do chaar din ki hai.
incidents.translation: Yes, for all these colleges I am telling you, the last date for all of them is just two to four days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the application deadlines are imminent without providing specific evidence or verification of these deadlines.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar still agar usme aapko mil jaata hai to aapko college wale connect kar lenge process ke liye.
incidents.translation: If you still get it, then the college will connect you for the process.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a high likelihood of admission without clarifying the actual admission criteria or the competitive nature of the process, which can mislead the student about their chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise the likelihood of admission, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan aaj last date hai extend kyunki already 25 na inka last date tha aur inhone extend kara tha kyunki kuch students ke na form na last time pe fill nahi ho paye the.
incidents.translation: Yes, today is the last date; it was already the 25th, and they extended it because some students couldn't fill the form last time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for application, which may not be entirely accurate or could be misleading, as it implies a fabricated scarcity to push the student to apply immediately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: UEM ka BPT ka highest salary gaya hai 22.60 LPA theek hai and seven LPA iska average hai.
incidents.translation: The highest salary for BPT at UEM is 22.60 LPA and the average is 7 LPA.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents placement claims that may not be verified or could be misleading, as they do not provide context or evidence for these figures, which could mislead the student about the college's actual placement performance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements regarding urgency and placement claims could mislead the student into making a hasty decision without adequate information.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur iski registration abki sirf 30 April tak hi open hai, matlab kal tak.
incidents.translation: And its registration is only open until April 30, meaning until tomorrow.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the registration is only open until the next day, which may not be true or may not be a significant deadline.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Scholarship up to 100% milta hai. Agar aap exam mein depend aap kaisa score kar rahe ho.
incidents.translation: Scholarship up to 100% is available. It depends on how you score in the exam.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that a 100% scholarship is possible without clarifying the conditions or likelihood of achieving such a score, which can mislead the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency and scholarship claims that could mislead the student regarding the actual admission process and financial implications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Uske baad jo bhi SRM ka hai, yeh bhi free hai aur Amity ka bhi free hai, ek do aur colleges hain.
incidents.translation: After that, whatever is for SRM, this is also free and Amity is also free, there are one or two more colleges.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the applications are free as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of matching the colleges with the student's profile and interests.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application aspect could mislead the student into prioritizing cost over suitability.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh NIIT aapka budget ke upar nahi jaa raha?
incidents.translation: So NIIT is not going beyond your budget?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, without adequately discussing the fit of the college with the student's profile and budget.

incidents.counsellor_quote: And placements highest salary they offered 80 lakhs and 92% batch place hue through college placement.
incidents.translation: And placements highest salary they offered 80 lakhs and 92% of the batch was placed through college placement.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents inflated placement figures without sufficient context, which could mislead the student into overestimating the college's placement success.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor provided some misleading information regarding college placements and emphasized free applications without discussing the student's specific needs adequately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap try kar sakte hai agar aapka sochiye ki achhe se ho gaya crack exam theek hai and aapko around 50, 60, 70% scholarship mil jati hai.
incidents.translation: So you can try if you think you can crack the exam well and you might get around 50, 60, 70% scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the potential for high scholarships without clarifying that these are contingent on exam performance, which could mislead the student into thinking they are guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap wo accept karte hai to aapko continue karna hai agar nahi karte to aap wahi pe reject kar sakte hai.
incidents.translation: If you accept it, you have to continue; if you don't, you can reject it right there.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement implies a lack of pressure in the admission process, which may not reflect the reality of college admissions, thus misleading the student about their options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarship potential and admission processes that could mislead the student regarding their actual chances and options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aur aapko placements wagera bhi yahan pe acchi mil jayegi. Around highest salary they offered 19 lakhs and 100% batch placed hua hai.
incidents.translation: and you will get good placements here. Around the highest salary they offered is 19 lakhs and 100% of the batch was placed.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that 100% of the batch was placed and mentions a highest salary of 19 lakhs without providing evidence or context, which could mislead the student about the actual placement scenario.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about placement statistics that could influence the student's decision without proper verification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: The seats are filling up very fast. I would suggest you complete the application today itself to secure your chance.
incidents.translation: The seats are filling up very fast. I would suggest you complete the application today itself to secure your chance.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's statement about seats filling up very fast lacks evidence and creates a sense of urgency that may not be justified, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used inflated urgency regarding seat availability, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki yeh October se start ho gaye the October November se inke application Bennett ke.
incidents.translation: because their applications for Bennett started in October and November.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that the application process has already started and suggests that the student should apply immediately, which can create unnecessary pressure without clear justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to agar aap Bennett University mein interested hain to matlab you want to do that?
incidents.translation: So if you are interested in Bennett University, it means you want to do that?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's phrasing suggests that admission is almost guaranteed if the student expresses interest, which can mislead the student about the admission process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed instances of creating urgency and implying guaranteed admission, which can mislead the student regarding the application process and their chances of acceptance.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To at least abhi aapka kuch paisa nahi lag raha hai to main aapko suggest karungi ki wo aap wo to fill kar hi lo.
incidents.translation: So at least since it won't cost you anything right now, I would suggest that you fill that out.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free application as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit for the student's profile or interests.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aapko main suggest karungi sabse pehle na aap Amity fill kar lo kyunki wo free hai, wo kabhi bhi paid ho sakta hai.
incidents.translation: So I would suggest that you fill out Amity first because it is free, it could become paid at any time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that the free application could change to a paid one, which pressures the student to act quickly without considering other options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the free application as a primary motivator and created a sense of urgency regarding the application process, which constitutes clear misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Sri Sri ka ye hai ki Sri Sri ka jo application hai na wo keval aaj ke liye aapka discounted chal raha hai Sri Sri ka.
incidents.translation: This is the case with Sri Sri that the application is currently available at a discount only for today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that the discounted application is only available for today, which may not be true and pressures the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding the application discount, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 60 seat hain yahan pe bas, toh thoda sa mushkil ho jaata hai.
incidents.translation: There are only 60 seats here, so it becomes a bit difficult.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency and scarcity regarding the limited number of seats, which could pressure the student to act quickly without verifying the actual situation.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding seat availability at RKDF University, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki seats limited hai, jab tak aap fees complete nahi karoge tab tak aapka fir nahi hoga kuch bhi, personal interview ya fir kuch bhi align.
incidents.translation: because seats are limited, until you complete the fees, nothing will happen for you, personal interview or anything will not align.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency regarding limited seats and the need to complete fees to proceed, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency that may mislead the student into thinking they must act quickly due to limited availability, which is a form of pressure-based selling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap abhi rehne do wo call attend mat karo.
incidents.translation: You should not attend that call right now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is discouraging the student from exploring other options or colleges, which is misleading as students should compare their choices.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi nahi ek hi hoga.
incidents.translation: No, no, it will be the same interview.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that all applications will lead to a single interview, which may not be accurate and can mislead the student about the admission process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's advice to avoid other calls and the assurance about a single interview for multiple applications can mislead the student regarding their options and the admission process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap kab tak apply kar denge matlab ye jo colleges ke baare mein maine bataya? Unhone aapke ek aur friend ka number diya hua tha to agar aap possible ho to unse bhi coordinate kar lijiyega, right? Kyunki application aap jitni jaldi fill karenge na, kyunki free application hai to ab ek saath mein bahut saare bachhe try kar rahe hain form fill karne ka, right?
incidents.translation: So when will you apply for the colleges I mentioned? They gave me another friend's number, so if possible, coordinate with them too, right? Because the sooner you fill the application, since it's free, many students are trying to fill the forms together, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the 'free application' as a primary reason to apply, which can pressure the student to act quickly without considering other important factors.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application creates a sense of urgency that may mislead the student into applying without fully considering their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: CGC bharo plus LPU. Punjab ho gaya, Delhi ho gaya, South side ho gaya.
incidents.translation: Fill CGC plus LPU. That covers Punjab, Delhi, and the South side.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting the student fill out applications for CGC and LPU without providing a clear rationale for why these specific colleges are the best fit, creating a sense of pressure to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: CGC isliye bhi better hai kyunki aaj ke liye coupon code hai. 1000 rupaye ka form hai, 500 rupaye ka padega.
incidents.translation: CGC is better because there is a coupon code today. The form costs 1000 rupees, but it will cost you 500.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes a coupon code as a reason to apply today, which creates a false sense of urgency without substantiating the claim that this is a unique or time-sensitive offer.

incidents.counsellor_quote: After IITs, NITs ke baad jo bhi university consider ho rahi hai, woh MIT WPU ho rahi hai.
incidents.translation: After IITs and NITs, the university being considered is MIT WPU.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that MIT WPU is on par with IITs and NITs without providing evidence, which can mislead the student into believing it has the same level of prestige and acceptance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed pressure tactics and made inflated claims about the urgency and prestige of certain colleges, which could mislead the student in their decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Inke paas sirf aaj ke hi liye coupon code inhone aaj ke liye bas provide kar diya hai ki aaj ke liye bache agar form bharenge to free of cost ho jayega.
incidents.translation: They have provided a coupon code only for today, that if students fill the form today, it will be free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the coupon code is only valid for today without providing evidence of this claim, which may pressure the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Usse koi matlab nahi main aapka karwa dunga waha pe theek hai.
incidents.translation: It doesn't matter, I will get you admitted there, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission to a college without any basis for this assurance, which is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about urgency and guaranteed admission, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Seats kaafi limited hain, so I would suggest ki aap application process aaj hi complete kar lein.
incidents.translation: Seats are quite limited, so I would suggest that you complete the application process today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency regarding limited seats without providing evidence, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency about limited seats, which could mislead the student into thinking they must act immediately without verifying the claim.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap ghar baithe hi ek entrance exam dena. Nahi bhi admission loge agar aapko lag raha hai aapki scholarship achhi nahi aayi, aap admission nahi le paoge.
incidents.translation: You can take the entrance exam from home. Even if you don't take admission, if you feel that your scholarship is not good, you won't be able to take admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free registration and suggests that taking the exam is a low-stakes action, which could pressure the student to apply without considering their actual interest in the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Lekin aaj hi kar lena kyunki yeh aaj hi ke liye free hai aur maine coupon code bhi apply kar diya hai isme theek hai?
incidents.translation: But do it today because it is free only for today and I have also applied the coupon code in it, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the free registration is only available for today, which may not be true and pressures the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics related to free registration and urgency, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Achha. To abhi matlab as such jaise maine aapko kuch... free of cost me application hai to aap wo bhi apply kar sakte hain kyunki uske liye na matlab bas ek interview wagera rehta hai zyada tar.
incidents.translation: Okay. So right now, as I told you, there are some... you can apply for free of cost because for that, there is usually just an interview.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free of cost' application as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the suitability of the colleges or courses for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ye college bahut badhiya hai. AICTE se approved hai aur dekhiye 4.1 ki inki rating hai.
incidents.translation: This college is very good. It is approved by AICTE and look, it has a rating of 4.1.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the college as a guaranteed good option without discussing the student's specific qualifications or fit, which can mislead the student into thinking admission is assured.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the appeal of free applications and made strong claims about the quality of colleges without adequately addressing the student's specific situation, which can lead to misinterpretation of the admission process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho main aapko yahi bolungi jab aapko saat option mil hi rahe hai free ke to kar lo, kya paisa thodi na ja raha hai isme.
incidents.translation: Look, I will tell you, when you have seven options available for free, then go for it, it's not like money is going anywhere.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free aspect of the application as the primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the decision should be based solely on the cost rather than the suitability of the colleges.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application option as a primary motivator for applying to multiple colleges raises concerns about the appropriateness of the advice given.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ek baar form fill kar lo, theek hai. And then dekho interview deke crack ho gaya to well and good hai.
incidents.translation: Just fill the form once, okay? And then see if you crack the interview, then it's well and good.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that filling the form and attending the interview guarantees a positive outcome, which can mislead the student into thinking admission is assured.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a statement that could mislead the student into believing that admission is guaranteed upon filling out the application and attending the interview.

incidents.counsellor_quote: sabse jo achhi baat hai is college ki ki ye bahut hi budget friendly option hai.
incidents.translation: The best thing about this college is that it is a very budget-friendly option.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the affordability of the college without providing substantial reasons for its fit for the student, which can mislead the student into thinking that cost is the primary factor.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapka overall jaise 12th mein ya diploma mein achha score rahega to ye jo college hai inka B.Tech CSE direct second year admission ke liye 27 LPA highest salary package gaya tha.
incidents.translation: If your overall score in 12th or diploma is good, this college has a highest salary package of 27 LPA for direct second-year admission in B.Tech CSE.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that a good score guarantees a high salary package, which can mislead the student into thinking that admission and salary are assured based on their previous scores.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims that could mislead the student regarding the affordability and potential outcomes of the colleges discussed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: so better try to fill if they are, see because what happens is Kumar, honestly speaking, if a student is looking out for any of the courses or any of the college, right? If there is something to pay then you can wait a minute, like wait for a day, wait for some hours. But if it is not asking anything to pay, you can at least initiate so that you can get that offer letter, right?
incidents.translation: So better try to fill if they are, see because what happens is Kumar, honestly speaking, if a student is looking out for any of the courses or any of the college, right? If there is something to pay then you can wait a minute, like wait for a day, wait for some hours. But if it is not asking anything to pay, you can at least initiate so that you can get that offer letter, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency to apply to GIET by suggesting that the student might lose the opportunity if they wait, despite the application being free. This creates a sense of pressure that is not warranted.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of urgency around the free application for GIET is misleading and creates unnecessary pressure on the student to apply quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Isme inka khud ka entrance exam hota hai jiske liye aap free mein apply kar sakte hain. Okay?
incidents.translation: It has its own entrance exam for which you can apply for free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free' application as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the college is a better option solely because of the free application, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student's needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap easily crack kar loge inka bhi entrance exam.
incidents.translation: So you can easily crack their entrance exam.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's statement implies a guarantee of success in the entrance exam based on the student's previous scores, which is misleading as it downplays the challenges of entrance exams.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims that could mislead the student regarding the ease of admission and the appeal of free applications, which could pressure the student into decisions based on these factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But abhi ke liye, as of now, abhi ke liye B.Tech mein iska form completely free hai.
incidents.translation: But for now, as of now, the application form for B.Tech is completely free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh usi mein woh log aapko confirm bata denge ki matlab aapka admission ho payega ya aapka admission nahi ho payega, woh aapko clear clear bata denge.
incidents.translation: They will clearly tell you whether you will be able to get admission or not.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the personal interview will guarantee clarity on admission status, which could mislead the student into thinking admission is assured based on this process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of the free application and the assurance regarding the admission process could mislead the student into thinking they have a higher chance of acceptance than may actually be the case.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan, abhi Computer Science branch mein kuch seats baaki hain, but admissions tezi se ho rahe hain. I suggest aap jald se jald application process complete kar lein.
incidents.translation: Yes, there are some seats left in the Computer Science branch, but admissions are happening quickly. I suggest you complete the application process as soon as possible.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that admissions are happening quickly without providing evidence of a deadline or actual scarcity, which can pressure the student to act hastily.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding seat availability without clear justification, which could mislead the student into making a rushed decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap ye samajh ke chalo ki scholarship to aapko milegi hi milegi.
incidents.translation: So you should understand that you will definitely get the scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees that the student will receive a scholarship, which is misleading as scholarships are typically contingent on various factors, including performance in exams.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab aap kahi jaoge nahi unhe ye cheez pata chal gayi to koi bhi aisi university ya koi bhi organization aisi nahi hogi jo apne nuksan me admission degi samajh rahe ho aap.
incidents.translation: If they find out that you are not fully interested in taking admission, no university or organization will admit you at a loss.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is discouraging the student from exploring other options by implying that universities will not admit them if they show any hesitation, which is a manipulative tactic.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about guaranteed scholarships and discouraged the student from exploring other options, which constitutes misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: seats limited hain
incidents.translation: seats are limited
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that seats are limited without providing evidence or context, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used a pressure tactic regarding limited seats, which could mislead the student into thinking they need to apply immediately without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur abhi seats bhi limited hi bachi hain, to aap jaldi se jaldi application form fill kar lijiye.
incidents.translation: And there are limited seats left, so please fill the application form as soon as possible.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim of limited seats creates a sense of urgency that may not be substantiated, pressuring the student to act quickly without providing evidence.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Interview clear karne ke baad aapko 10% ka scholarship mil jayega total fees par.
incidents.translation: After clearing the interview, you will get a 10% scholarship on the total fees.
incidents.why_misselling: The promise of a scholarship conditioned on clearing an interview may mislead the student into thinking it's guaranteed, which could be seen as bait.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used inflated urgency regarding seat availability and made a conditional scholarship promise, both of which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap thoda sa ye dhyan rakhiyega, jo colleges maine aapko Delhi NCR ke bataye hain aur wo UPES maine aapko bataya, wo main share karti hoon.
incidents.translation: So please keep in mind the colleges I have mentioned in Delhi NCR and the one I mentioned about UPES, I will share that.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the student needs to act quickly to secure admission, which can pressure the student into making hasty decisions without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: UPES to ma'am kaafi achha hai, ma'am wo to tier one mein aata hai, matlab top 50 mein aata hai, uske liye to apply karna hi chahiye.
incidents.translation: UPES is quite good, ma'am, it falls under tier one, meaning it is in the top 50, you should definitely apply for it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents UPES as a guaranteed good option without discussing the student's specific profile or the competitive nature of admissions, which can mislead the student about their chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed signs of urgency and made strong recommendations without sufficient context, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply to specific colleges.

incidents.counsellor_quote: actually in under BSc na these colleges are going to shut down their application on 30th, like after three days.
incidents.translation: actually under BSc these colleges are going to shut down their application on the 30th, like after three days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that applications will close soon without providing evidence or context, which may pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: you do not have to pay anything, do you want to apply for that those also?
incidents.translation: you do not have to pay anything, do you want to apply for those also?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying to multiple campuses incurs no cost, which could mislead the student into thinking there are no financial implications at all, potentially downplaying the overall fees involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and downplay potential costs, which could pressure the student into applying without fully understanding the implications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh dekho abhi 16th rank hai as per Times all over India B.Tech program ke liye, 4.1 ka rating hai, toh yeh bhi ek achha campus hai agar hum option rakhna chahte hain toh.
incidents.translation: So see, it is currently ranked 16th as per Times all over India for the B.Tech program, with a rating of 4.1, so this is also a good campus if we want to keep it as an option.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the ranking and rating as a strong reason to consider Amity University, which could mislead the student into thinking that these metrics guarantee a superior education or placement outcomes without sufficient context.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi uska koi entrance exam nahi hai, JEE Mains ke score ke basis pe aur plus aapka jo qualifying exam hai uske basis pe selection aapka rehta hai.
incidents.translation: No, there is no entrance exam for that, selection is based on the JEE Mains score and your qualifying exam.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that admission is straightforward based solely on JEE Mains scores, which may not reflect the actual competitive nature of the admissions process, potentially misleading the student about their chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of the university's ranking and the admissions process could mislead the student into overestimating their chances and the quality of the program.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka na dekho kya hota hai basically agar aap ek location ka fill karoge to aapko second form na aap upar se change kar sakte ho to usme aapko direct hi khud change kar loge aapko dobara dobara sari cheeze add nahi karni hogi bas ek baar aapko add karni hogi second time pe aap apna campus change karke bhar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: Look at Amity, basically if you fill one location, you can change the second form from above, so you can directly change it yourself without having to add everything again, you just need to add it once, and the second time you can change your campus and fill it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the ease of applying to Amity as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's merits or fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: mai bhi apni side se try karungi ki aapko mil jaye and second cheez ki aap bhi try kar do ki aapko mil jaye to try karna ki iska application bas ye galti mat karna ki aap apne number se bhar do kyuki na fir agar aapne apne number se bhar diya to I cannot help you.
incidents.translation: I will also try from my side to ensure you get in, and you should also try to ensure you get in, just don't make the mistake of filling it with your own number because if you do, I cannot help you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting a strong likelihood of admission to PDPU without clear evidence, which can mislead the student into thinking admission is guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed instances of misrepresentation regarding the application process and admission likelihood, which could mislead the student into making decisions based on inflated expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki wahi hai na ki abhi to. Haan mereko actually jinhone bataya na wo keh rahe hain ki hai na abhi agar by maan lo first list mein na aaye to bhi last tak bhi aa jaata hai iska.
incidents.translation: Because that's it, right? Yes, actually those who told me said that even if you don't make it to the first list, you can still get in till the last.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that admission is likely even if the student does not make it to the first list, which can mislead the student into thinking they have a guaranteed chance of admission.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aap form bhar do agar cut off wagera nikal jaati hai to last mein mil jayega to bhi theek hai.
incidents.translation: If you fill the form and the cut-off comes out, you can still get in at the last moment, that's fine too.
incidents.why_misselling: This creates a sense of urgency to fill the form under the false impression that admission is still possible at the last moment, which may not be true.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the likelihood of admission and created a false sense of urgency regarding the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki phase two mein fir admission ka process agar aap dekhoge na, wo thoda sa aapko hard padega us time pe.
incidents.translation: Because if you look at the admission process in phase two, it will be a bit harder for you at that time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that waiting for phase two will make the admission process significantly more difficult, creating unnecessary urgency without clear justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki aap bhi jante ho us time pe to fir college ke liye admission, admission hi aapke chalenge.
incidents.translation: Because you know that at that time, the admission process will be ongoing for colleges.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement suggests that the student will miss out on opportunities if they do not act quickly, which is a pressure tactic.

incidents.counsellor_quote: NIT to kafi achha college hai aur AKTU ka college hai wo to aur aap bhi shayad jante ho us campus ke bare mein to achhe se.
incidents.translation: NIT is a very good college and it is an AKTU college, and you might already know well about that campus.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a strong likelihood of admission to NIT without discussing the student's qualifications or the competitive nature of the admissions process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics to create urgency and made claims about admission likelihood that could mislead the student regarding their options and the admissions process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Chandigarh University ki kal last date hai aisi hi Chandigarh University CGC University ki kal last date hai aaj.
incidents.translation: Chandigarh University has a last date tomorrow, similarly, CGC University has a last date today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor fabricates a deadline for CGC University, implying urgency to push the student to apply without evidence of an actual deadline.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 100% aapko B.Tech CSE AI ML mil jayega itne percentage pe.
incidents.translation: You will definitely get B.Tech CSE AI ML at this percentage.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission based on the student's percentage, which is a blatant false claim.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding application deadlines and guaranteed admission based on the student's scores.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh maan lo agar Gurgaon wala bol de aapko Amity ki 100% aapko fees mein hum ek saal ki 100% de rahe hain scholarship, matlab ki fees hi nahi deni hai aapko ek saal ki, theek hai?
incidents.translation: So suppose if the one in Gurgaon tells you that they are giving you 100% scholarship on the fees for one year, it means you don't have to pay any fees for one year, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the scholarship as a guaranteed 100% fee waiver for one year without clarifying that such offers may be conditional or not universally applicable, which can mislead the student into thinking they will not have to pay any fees.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan minimum yeh toh milna hi milna hai Amity ka aur highest jo hai iska toh 50 plus jata hai, 60 plus, 50 plus.
incidents.translation: Yes, this minimum is definitely what you will get from Amity, and the highest goes above 50, 60 plus, above 50.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor provides placement figures that may not be verified or accurate, implying guaranteed outcomes which can mislead the student regarding their job prospects after graduation.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about guaranteed scholarships and placement figures that could mislead the student regarding the actual financial obligations and job prospects.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ye abhi jo link maine active kiya hai ye sirf 12 minute ke liye hai.
incidents.translation: This link that I have activated is only valid for 12 minutes.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor fabricates a sense of urgency by claiming the link is only valid for 12 minutes without any evidence, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a false sense of urgency, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi Shiksha ki site se maine abhi check kiya hai, offer hai. Free mein application form fill ho raha hai, koi payment nahi karni.
incidents.translation: I just checked on Shiksha's site, there is an offer. The application form is being filled for free, no payment needs to be made.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the college is a good fit solely based on the lack of application fees, rather than on the college's merits.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 87% ka placement record hai inka. Theek hai? 60 LPA ka highest hai, average 6 lakh ke aas paas ka hai.
