incidents.counsellor_quote: To wo uske basis pe na aapko kuch scholarship grant zarur karenge, theek hai?
incidents.translation: Based on that, they will definitely grant you some scholarship, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests a guarantee of scholarship based on the student's profile, which may not be assured and can mislead the student into thinking they will receive financial aid.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited pressure tactics regarding deadlines and made promises about scholarships that may not be guaranteed, which can mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: scholarship bahut achhi milti hai aur yahan pe milti hi milti hai.
incidents.translation: Scholarships are very good here and they are always available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim that scholarships are always available is misleading, as it implies a guarantee of financial aid without acknowledging the conditions or competition involved.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Highest package yahan ka bata deta hoon. BBA mein highest package jo last year ka gaya hai woh 32 LPA ka gaya hai as a fresher.
incidents.translation: Let me tell you the highest package here. The highest package for BBA last year was 32 LPA as a fresher.
incidents.why_misselling: The claim about the highest package is potentially misleading as it does not clarify the context or the number of students achieving such packages, which could create unrealistic expectations.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarships and placement packages that could mislead the student regarding the actual opportunities available.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aaj ke liye coupon code activated hai kyunki first come first serve slot hai to aaj last date hai, hai na, first come first serve ki.
incidents.translation: The coupon code is activated for today because it's a first come first serve slot, so today is the last date.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a false sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for the coupon code without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi, main bol to raha hoon, kahin nahi jaana padega beta, kuch nahi karna padega, online hi hoga, don't worry.
incidents.translation: No, I'm telling you, you won't have to go anywhere, you won't have to do anything, it will all be online, don't worry.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor assures the student that there will be no need for any physical presence or additional steps, which may not be true and can mislead the student about the admission process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that created a false sense of urgency and made misleading promises about the admission process, which could pressure the student into applying without fully understanding the implications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aaj last date hai shayad form ki
incidents.translation: today is probably the last date for the form
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that today is the last date for the application form without verifying the actual deadline, which misleads the student into feeling pressured to apply immediately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar suppose that ki agar nahi CU mein ho pa raha hai utna ya phir MIT Pune mein hamara utna achha marks nahi aaya hai... to at least agar hum enter karna chahe us college mein to hum enter kar sakte hain.
incidents.translation: if suppose we are not able to get into CU or MIT Pune with our marks... at least if we want to enter that college, we can enter.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student can still enter the college regardless of their entrance exam performance, which is misleading as admission is typically contingent on merit.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited pressure tactics regarding application deadlines and made misleading claims about guaranteed admission based on entrance exam performance.

incidents.counsellor_quote: toh aap chahe toh hamare paas jaise kuch forms hain jaise abhi free of cost hain jisme aapne already teen se teen form fill kar chuke ho, toh almost aapke paas jo hai do se teen form jo hain abhi yahan pe fresh aapko yeh offer provide ho sakta hai, toh isme aap free of cost fill kar sakte hain form theek hai?
incidents.translation: So you can choose from some forms we have, which are currently free of cost, and since you have already filled three forms, you can fill two to three more fresh forms here, so you can fill these forms free of cost, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the 'free of cost' aspect as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the suitability or benefits of the colleges, which can mislead the student into thinking that the cost is the only consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application forms could lead the student to prioritize cost over the actual fit of the colleges, which is a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: beta yeh cheez jo aapko provide ho raha hai na, yeh IEM wala, yeh aapko tab provide ho raha hai na jab aapne yahan pe teen paid form fill kare hain, tab jaakar ke Shiksha ki taraf se aapko kuch free of cost yahan forms provide hua hai.
incidents.translation: This thing that is being provided to you, this IEM one, is being provided to you only when you have filled three paid forms here, then only will you get some free forms from Shiksha.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that the free application forms are contingent on filling paid forms, which creates a misleading incentive to apply without proper justification for the value of the free forms.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh isse ekdum last jab hamare paas kuch option nahi bachega, toh aap Amity kar lena.
incidents.translation: So, when we have no options left, then you can take Amity.
incidents.why_misselling: This suggests that Amity is a fallback option without acknowledging the student's preferences, which can mislead the student into thinking it's a guaranteed option when they have no other choices.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading impression about the conditions for free applications and the certainty of admission to Amity, which could pressure the student into decisions without fully informed consent.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur iski jo seats hain wo limited seats hain, to aap jaldi se jaldi iska application form fill kar lijiye.
incidents.translation: And the seats for this are limited, so please fill out the application form as soon as possible.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by claiming that seats are limited without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into applying quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about limited seats appears to be an attempt to create urgency without substantiation, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Isme mention bhi hai inka A plus neck hai and rank inka two dikha raha hai website pe and 92 in NIRF ranking hai inki engineering ki.
incidents.translation: It is mentioned that they have an A plus accreditation and their rank is showing as two on the website, and their NIRF ranking in engineering is 92.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the NIRF ranking without verifying its accuracy, which can mislead the student into believing the college has a higher standing than it actually does.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding the NIRF ranking of Chitkara University, which could influence the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But main wahi keh rahi hu aap fir Sonipat wala kar lo na, Sonipat ki to fees bhi kam hai usme agar aapko scholarship mil bhi jayegi jaise Sonipat ki na to proper 12 lakh fees hai.
incidents.translation: But I am saying the same, you should go for Sonipat, the fees for Sonipat is also lower, and if you get a scholarship, the proper fees will be 12 lakh.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that admission to the Sonipat campus is assured if the student applies, which can be misleading as it does not account for the competitive nature of admissions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But usme maine aapko kaha tha ki scholarship mil jayegi na, abhi SRM ka test bhi ho raha hai aapka to scholarship wala test aap kar lo.
incidents.translation: But I told you that you will get a scholarship, and your SRM test is also happening, so you should take the scholarship test.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that a scholarship is guaranteed, which may not be the case and can mislead the student into thinking they will receive financial aid without proper basis.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims regarding guaranteed admission and scholarships that could mislead the student about the actual admission process and financial commitments.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap uske usme bhi jaake dekhoge jab BM Munjal ka website bhi check karenge to B.Tech courses mein jin bachchon ke 98 plus percentile hain, unhe mil rahe hain, theek hai?
incidents.translation: You will also see on the BM Munjal website that students with 98 plus percentile are getting scholarships, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that students with a specific percentile are guaranteed scholarships, which can mislead the student into thinking they will receive similar benefits without acknowledging the competitive nature and conditions of the scholarship.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Paanch din bhi bahut hote hain. Paanch din mein kitne bachche application daal rahe hain.
incidents.translation: Five days is also a lot. In five days, how many students are applying?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the student must act quickly due to high competition, which may not be substantiated by actual data or evidence.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed instances of misselling by implying guaranteed scholarships based on percentile and creating an inflated sense of urgency for applications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: UEM ka application absolutely free hai aapko kuch nahi karna hai aapko kahin kuch pay nahi karna hai.
incidents.translation: The UEM application is absolutely free, you don't have to do anything, you don't have to pay anything.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the application is the most important factor rather than the suitability of the college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application process for UEM and Amity University could mislead the student into prioritizing cost over the quality and fit of the colleges.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap hamare through apply karte hain, to aapko 10% ka waiver mil jayega total fees par.
incidents.translation: If you apply through us, you will get a 10% waiver on the total fees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the 10% waiver as a special benefit for applying through Shiksha.com, which may create an impression that this is a guaranteed discount, potentially misleading the student about the actual availability or conditions of the waiver.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But iske liye seats limited hain, to aapko jaldi apply karna hoga.
incidents.translation: But for this, seats are limited, so you need to apply quickly.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency regarding limited seats without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about the waiver and limited seats create a misleading sense of urgency and potential benefits that may not be fully accurate.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Admission to ho jayega na mera? Haan don't worry.
incidents.translation: My admission will happen, right? Yes, don't worry.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is assuring the student that admission is guaranteed without any basis, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Iske baad kyunki iski seat bhi kam hai aur secondary cheez iska last date beta 13th April hai.
incidents.translation: After this, because the seats are limited and the last date is 13th April.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by stating that seats are limited and a deadline is approaching, which may not be true.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about guaranteed admission and created a false sense of urgency regarding application deadlines.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka form abhi chal raha hai, usme bhi jo aapke wahan ke student hai na, aapke most of the slots pe jo Amity ka form hai na woh aapka free hai.
incidents.translation: The Amity form is currently open, and for most of the slots for your area, the Amity form is free for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits, which can mislead the student into thinking the college is a better option solely due to the free application.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application as a key selling point raises concerns about the appropriateness of the advice given.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki close ho rahe hain application, Kite ki bhi about to close hai, usme bhi zyada time nahi hai.
incidents.translation: because the applications are closing, Kite is also about to close, there isn't much time left.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the application deadlines are imminent without providing concrete evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to agar aap seventy five score kar loge to aap Bennett ka form bhar sakte ho. Wahan pe aapko scholarship to shayad nahi milegi itne score pe but yes interview aur twelfth ke score ke basis pe ya tenth ke score ke basis pe aapko admission zaroor mil jayega.
incidents.translation: So if you can score seventy five, you can fill the form for Bennett. You might not get a scholarship with that score, but yes, based on the interview and twelfth or tenth score, you will definitely get admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that admission is guaranteed based on the student's past scores, which is misleading as admission decisions depend on various factors and cannot be assured.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics regarding application deadlines and made misleading claims about guaranteed admission based on the student's scores.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi yehi hai ek do din hi aur hai bas.
incidents.translation: Right now, there are just one or two days left.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency about the registration deadline without providing concrete evidence, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aur socho isme scholarship mil gayi to kind of free hoga aapka ye.
incidents.translation: And think, if you get a scholarship in this, it will be kind of free for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student could receive a scholarship that would make the program effectively free, which may not be guaranteed and is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency and suggests potential scholarships that may not be guaranteed, which can mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Sage Sunstone ek tarike se matlab placement ki guarantee bhi deta hai.
incidents.translation: Sage Sunstone provides a kind of guarantee for placements.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim that Sage Sunstone guarantees placements is misleading, as no college can guarantee job placements due to various external factors.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a misleading claim about placement guarantees, which could unduly influence the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh uska mere paas free wala coupon hai matlab waise toh uska application 1100 ka hai but still main aapko coupon de dungi toh woh free ho jayega teeno campuses ke liye.
incidents.translation: So I have a free coupon for that, meaning normally its application is 1100, but still I will give you a coupon so it will be free for all three campuses.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary incentive without adequately discussing the college's merits or fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh Chandigarh University aap consider kar sakte hain it's matlab better hai iska placements wagera bhi and inme aapko scholarship bhi achi mil jaati hai.
incidents.translation: So you can consider Chandigarh University, it's better in terms of placements and you also get good scholarships.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that Chandigarh University has better placements without providing specific evidence or context, which could mislead the student about the actual outcomes.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on free applications and vague claims about placements may mislead the student regarding the true value and fit of the colleges discussed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka to dekho mere paas na abhi coupon bhi hai to main coupon bhej dungi to uska free ho jayega Amity ka to.
incidents.translation: Look, I have a coupon for Amity, so I will send the coupon, and it will be free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is using the free application as a primary reason to encourage the student to apply to Amity, rather than discussing the college's merits or fit for the student's profile.

incidents.counsellor_quote: jitna aap late karoge na usme aapko fir scholarship kam ho jayegi aapki.
incidents.translation: The later you delay, the lesser your scholarship will be.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency regarding scholarship availability without clear evidence that this is true, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that created urgency and emphasized free applications as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student regarding the true value of the colleges discussed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: college ke application bahut saare aa chuke hain already. And college ka jo applications hai wo closed bhi ho jayega this most probably this do teen din par last date hoga iska.
incidents.translation: Many applications for the college have already come in. And the college applications will also close, most probably the last date will be in the next two to three days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the application deadline is imminent without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar 12th mein aap above 70% laate ho to there are chances ke yahan pe admission aapka ho jayega.
incidents.translation: If you score above 70% in 12th, there are chances that you will get admission here.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a guarantee of admission based on a specific score without acknowledging other factors that may influence the admission process, which is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited clear instances of misselling by creating a false sense of urgency and overpromising admission based on the student's scores.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Application abhi sabhi colleges ke live chal rahe hai to first jo aapka abhi priority hona chahiye ki at least paanch chhe college mein apna application karke chalo.
incidents.translation: Applications are currently live for all colleges, so your priority should be to apply to at least five to six colleges.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the student must apply to multiple colleges immediately to avoid missing out, which can pressure the student into hasty decisions without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Matlab ye hai ki agar aap student padhne wale ho, skillful ho to aisa nahi hai ki aapko option nahi milega, matlab aapko milega hi milega.
incidents.translation: This means that if you are a studious and skillful student, it is not that you won't have options; you will definitely have options.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's statement guarantees that the student will have options for admission based on their skills, which is misleading as admission outcomes can vary widely and are not guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited pressure tactics and made misleading claims about admission outcomes, which could lead the student to make rushed decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: seats bhi limited hi hain, to aap form fill kar lijiye.
incidents.translation: Seats are also limited, so please fill the form.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that seats are limited without providing evidence or context, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about limited seats creates an unnecessary sense of urgency, which can mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Admission to aapko waise bhi mil jayega usme koi dikkat nahi hai lekin jaise abhi aap registration karoge depend karega ki kya hai, kya process aage hoga, woh admission wala process to waise aapko university hi detail mein share karte hain.
incidents.translation: You will definitely get admission, there is no problem in that, but it will depend on the registration you do and what the process will be, the admission process will be shared in detail by the university.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that admission is guaranteed without acknowledging the actual requirements or competition, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Bilkul yeh yeh sahi keh rahe ho aap.
incidents.translation: You are absolutely right.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is reinforcing the student's concern about missing out on seats, which creates unnecessary urgency without factual basis.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about guaranteed admission and created a sense of urgency without proper justification, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho agar wo by chance bolte hai na ki schedule karwane ke liye kuch slots dete hai to aap at least do teen din ka gap leke chalna, theek hai?
incidents.translation: Look, if they happen to say that they provide some slots for scheduling, then you should at least take a gap of two to three days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the student should act quickly to secure a slot, which may not be based on actual availability, creating a sense of pressure.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar dekho unhone university ne yeh option de rakha hai ki interview ke basis pe hi aapko admission mil raha hai to koi to reason hoga na.
incidents.translation: If the university has given this option that you will get admission based on the interview, then there must be some reason for it.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement suggests that admission is almost guaranteed based on the interview, which can mislead the student into thinking that they are assured a place without considering other factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi se yeh hoga aap jaise registration bhi kar lete ho na to aapko bhale hi aap admission apne result ke baad lo na lekin aapko fees utni hi offer karegi because aap registered kar chuke ho.
incidents.translation: If you register now, even if you take admission after your result, you will still be offered the same fees because you have already registered.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates a false sense of urgency by implying that registering now will secure the same fee later, which may not be true and can pressure the student into making a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a sense of urgency and imply guaranteed admission based on the interview, which can mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko isliye zaroori hoga kyunki abhi inke kuch dino mein applications close ho jayenge, theek hai?
incidents.translation: It is necessary for you because their applications will close in a few days, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that applications will close soon without providing any evidence or specific deadline, which may pressure the student to apply hastily.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aapko lag raha hai ki ye teen college hai jisme, inme se kisi ek mein to jaana hi hai, to aap uska application fill karke rakh do kyunki token amount wali cheez to baad mein jayegi na
incidents.translation: If you feel that these three colleges are the ones you definitely want to go to, then fill out the application for one of them because the token amount can be dealt with later.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student is guaranteed a spot in the college if they apply, which is misleading as admission is not guaranteed solely based on application.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics by suggesting urgency in applying and implied guaranteed admission, which are clear cases of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: admission to aapko har haal mein mil jayega.
incidents.translation: You will definitely get admission in any case.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission, which is a blatant false claim as admission is not guaranteed for every applicant.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi to kar raha hoon main, itna jaldi to kyun ho jayegi full?
incidents.translation: I am doing it now, why would it fill up so quickly?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor downplays the urgency of applying, suggesting that seats will not fill up quickly, which may mislead the student about the actual competition and availability.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about guaranteed admission and downplayed the urgency of applying, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to then fir aapko thodi bahut scholarship mil jati hai college ki side se kyunki ye aapka 90 plus hai na academics 10th mein to us basis pe mil jayegi aapko.
incidents.translation: then you will get some scholarship from the college side because you have 90 plus in your 10th academics, so you will get it based on that.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student will receive a scholarship based on their academic performance without clarifying that this is contingent on admission and not guaranteed, which can mislead the student into thinking they are assured financial aid.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the availability of scholarships based on academic performance without proper context, which could lead the student to have unrealistic expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab unke paas bhale hi 100 application ho ya 200 application ho jitne zyada application honge unka number baccho ka vaise hi aayega. Ab suppose unke paas total 120 seats hain B.Tech mein.
incidents.translation: Even if they have 100 applications or 200 applications, the number of students will increase accordingly. Now suppose they have a total of 120 seats in B.Tech.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency by suggesting that the number of applications directly correlates with the limited number of seats, creating unnecessary pressure on the student to apply quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To abhi yahan pe seats almost 60 ke around hain 60 to 80 ke around seats hain B.Tech CSE mein for inke session ki 2026 ki.
incidents.translation: So currently there are around 60 to 80 seats available in B.Tech CSE for their 2026 session.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the limited number of seats to create a sense of urgency, suggesting that the student should apply immediately, which is a pressure tactic rather than providing genuine reasons for fit.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics related to application urgency and limited seat availability, which could mislead the student into feeling rushed to apply.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap ye bhi kar sakte ho ki for the time being aap kuch aise colleges mein bhi apply kar sakte ho Atish. Jisse ki aapko kya hoga ki aapko ye pata lag jayega ki aapka Sage mein, Medi-Caps mein ya fir whatever jo bhi college hai us college mein admission kaise hona hai.
incidents.translation: You can also do this, for the time being, you can apply to some colleges like this, Atish. This way, you will know how the admission process works in Sage, Medi-Caps, or whatever college.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is encouraging the student to apply to colleges under the pretext of gaining experience in the admission process, while emphasizing that the application is free, which could mislead the student into thinking there are no consequences or commitments involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on free applications and gaining experience could mislead the student into applying without fully understanding the implications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur seats bhi limited hain, to aap jaldi se jaldi apply kar dijiye.
incidents.translation: And there are limited seats, so please apply as soon as possible.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency about limited seats without providing evidence, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about limited seats creates a sense of urgency that may not be justified, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan Gurgaon wale campus ka hamare paas coupon code hai aaj ke liye main uska free mein fill karwa dungi aapka but iska abhi aapko pay karna padega iska.
incidents.translation: Yes, for the Gurgaon campus we have a coupon code for today, I will fill it for you for free, but you will have to pay for this one.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a selling point while still requiring payment for another application, which can mislead the student into thinking they are getting a better deal than they actually are.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi hai isme do minute ka time hai.
incidents.translation: There is only two minutes time left for this.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency without clear justification, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that could mislead the student regarding the urgency and benefits of the application process, particularly emphasizing free applications while still requiring payment for others.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aap mujhe bata do abhi thodi der mein, main aapko share kar dungi details.
incidents.translation: let me know if you want to apply now, I will share the details with you shortly.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free and encourages the student to apply without providing substantial reasons for the college's fit, which can be seen as a pressure tactic.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki kal Amity Gurgaon ki last date hai to Amity Gurgaon mein to aap apply kar sakte ho
incidents.translation: because tomorrow is the last date for Amity Gurgaon, so you can apply to Amity Gurgaon.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating a deadline that pressures the student to act quickly without allowing for proper consideration of options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included pressure tactics regarding application deadlines and emphasized the free application process without adequately discussing the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi, matlab baad mein to kuch bhi nahi fill kar sakte ho, sab process aapko step by step hi fill karna padega.
incidents.translation: No, it means you can't fill anything later, you have to fill the entire process step by step.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the student must complete the application process immediately, which may not be true and pressures the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's insistence on immediate action creates undue pressure on the student, which is a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi nahi, Amity ka koi charges nahi hai, don't worry.
incidents.translation: No, no, there are no charges for Amity, don't worry.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that there are no charges for the Amity registration, which may mislead the student into thinking there are no fees at all, potentially hiding other costs associated with the admission process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka main aapka alag alag location ka saara fill kar dungi, theek hai?
incidents.translation: I will fill in all the locations for Amity for you, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's assurance to fill in all locations for Amity implies a strong likelihood of admission without discussing the competitive nature of the process, which could mislead the student about their chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding fees and the admission process, which could create false expectations for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Scholarship milne ke hundred percent chances hote hain, university aapko bahut option deti hai scholarship ke liye.
incidents.translation: There are hundred percent chances of getting a scholarship, the university provides you many options for scholarships.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim of 'hundred percent chances' for scholarships is misleading as it implies guaranteed outcomes without acknowledging the actual eligibility criteria and competition involved.

incidents.counsellor_quote: First come first serve slot abhi open hai.
incidents.translation: The first come first serve slot is currently open.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates a sense of urgency that may not be substantiated, as it implies limited availability without clear evidence of a deadline or scarcity.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding scholarship guarantees and created a sense of urgency without clear justification, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: currently if you apply for Amity Mumbai na, so application fees is also waived off
incidents.translation: currently if you apply for Amity Mumbai, the application fees is also waived off
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fee as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking this is the most important factor rather than the college's fit for their needs.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the waived application fee for Amity University could mislead the student into prioritizing cost over suitability.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aapko humne do teen colleges decide kare. Ek Galgotia ho gaya aur humne kaun kaun se kare hain?
incidents.translation: So we have decided on two or three colleges for you. One is Galgotia and what else have we done?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free application process as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the application is the most important factor rather than the suitability of the colleges.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapka 12th ka score above 70% hai aur CUET aapne likha hua hai to for sure aapko admission mil jayega.
incidents.translation: If your 12th score is above 70% and you have written CUET, then for sure you will get admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission based on the student's scores and CUET results, which is misleading as admission is not guaranteed and depends on various factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To main aapko suggest kar rahi hu ki at least ek do colleges ke jo free hain wo to hum bhar hi denge.
incidents.translation: So I am suggesting you that at least we should fill out the forms for one or two colleges that are free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the student should apply to free colleges immediately, implying that there is a limited time to act, which can pressure the student into making quick decisions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited multiple instances of misselling by emphasizing free applications as a primary reason to apply, guaranteeing admission based on scores, and creating a sense of urgency around the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab yeh jo aapka session bacha hai na, yeh last session hai aapka. Iske baad aapka koi bhi private colleges me admission mil paana impossible hai.
incidents.translation: Now this remaining session is your last session. After this, it will be impossible for you to get admission in any private colleges.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a false sense of urgency by claiming that this is the last opportunity for admission, which may not be true and pressures the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Beta abhi kuch bhi mat karna abhi, theek hai? Yeh jo college hai na aapke, yeh woh colleges hain jinke, jinke number ke based par admission hota hai, theek hai?
incidents.translation: Beta, don't do anything right now, okay? This college is one of those colleges where admission is based on your number.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application form is free as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's merits or fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics to create urgency and emphasized the free application as a key reason to apply, which constitutes misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki last date hai iski aaj.
incidents.translation: because today is the last date for this.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for the application, which may not be true or verified, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's claim about the urgency of the application deadline is misleading, as it pressures the student to apply without confirming the actual deadline.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aaj ke liye uski application ke koi charges nahi hain, theek hai?
incidents.translation: Today there are no charges for the application, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application may mislead the student into applying without considering other important factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki iska aaj bhar hi free of cost hai, kal se phir 1100 iske charges lag jayenge.
incidents.translation: because today it is free of cost, from tomorrow there will be charges of 1100.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the application is free today but will incur a fee tomorrow, which may pressure the student to apply without considering other options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the urgency of applying for free creates pressure on the student, which is a clear case of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab ek campus ke andar kitni seats hogi zyada se zyada? Hogi mushkil se 150 ke kareeb ho jayegi. Ab apply karne wale do teen lakh bachhe hain.
incidents.translation: Now, how many seats will there be in one campus at most? There will be hardly around 150. Now, there are two to three lakh students applying.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that there are very few seats available compared to a large number of applicants, which may not be substantiated.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about seat scarcity create undue pressure on the student to apply quickly, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Lucknow wale ka free of cost form hai abhi aur yeh kab band ho jaye pata nahi.
incidents.translation: The form for Lucknow is free of cost right now, and I don't know when it will close.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies an urgency regarding the application deadline without providing evidence of a real deadline, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: jitna jaldi aap apply karoge utna jaldi aapka selection hoga aur jitna late karoge utna closing ho jayega, aapka admission bhi nahi ho payega.
incidents.translation: The sooner you apply, the faster your selection will be, and the later you do, the more it will close, and you won't be able to get admission.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates a sense of urgency and pressure to apply immediately, suggesting that delay will result in losing the opportunity, which may not be substantiated.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics to create a sense of urgency regarding the application process for Amity University, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: KJ Somaiya all time best hai. Done, koi competition hi nahi hai.
incidents.translation: KJ Somaiya is all time best. Done, there is no competition.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's statement implies that KJ Somaiya is the best option without acknowledging the student's specific needs or other viable alternatives, which can mislead the student into thinking there are no other suitable colleges.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur jo highest gaya tha wo 85 lakh rupees ka tha.
incidents.translation: And the highest package was 85 lakh rupees.
incidents.why_misselling: Claiming a specific highest package without context or verification can mislead students about the realistic outcomes they might expect, especially if such figures are not typical for most graduates.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made strong claims about KJ Somaiya's superiority and placement statistics that could mislead the student regarding their options and potential outcomes.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Mam dekhiye Amity Lucknow ka jo application hai uski koi charges aapko nahi pay karne rahenge wo aap without paying anything submit kar sakte hain.
incidents.translation: Ma'am, you see, for the Amity Lucknow application, you won't have to pay any charges; you can submit it without paying anything.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that this is the most important factor rather than the college's fit for their needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To kya rehta hai na seats agar full honi shuru ho jaati hain to fir uske baad college mein maan lijiye koi ek particular branch aapko bhi pata hai aaj kal bachche CSE aur AI ML sabse zyada focus karte hain.
incidents.translation: So what happens is that if the seats start filling up, then after that, for a particular branch, you know that nowadays students are focusing the most on CSE and AI ML.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency regarding seat availability, suggesting that the student must act quickly to secure a spot, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that could mislead the student regarding the urgency and importance of applying, focusing on the free application as a primary motivator.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To isme B.Tech ki seats jo hain sabse pehle full hoti hain.
incidents.translation: In this, the B.Tech seats fill up first.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency by stating that B.Tech seats fill up first without providing evidence, which pressures the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To unka sabka admission jo tha done ho gaya tha admission.
incidents.translation: So all their admissions were done.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that admissions are guaranteed for those who apply early, which is misleading and creates pressure on the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aapko woh ek din nikalna padega time ki main university campus chala jaata hoon, dekh kar aa jaata hoon kaisa hai.
incidents.translation: You will need to take a day to visit the university campus and see how it is.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor glosses over the high fees of K.R. Mangalam University while emphasizing the need to visit the campus, which may mislead the student about the affordability of the college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed pressure tactics and misleading claims regarding admissions and urgency, which could sway the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki iska na aaj phase one close ho jayega Chandigarh University ka.
incidents.translation: because today is the last day for phase one of Chandigarh University.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last day for phase one without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to usme bachho ko up to 100% tak scholarship mil sakti hai.
incidents.translation: students can get up to 100% scholarship in that.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student could receive a significant scholarship, which may not be guaranteed and is contingent upon applying, thus enticing the student without clear conditions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency and scholarship claims to push the student towards applying, which could mislead the student regarding the actual situation.

incidents.counsellor_quote: manipul ka na ekdam aap confirm mat ho ke chalo ki matlab ho hi jayega na.
incidents.translation: Don't be too sure about Manipal, it will definitely happen.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a guarantee of admission to Manipal, which is misleading and creates false confidence for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement suggests a certainty of admission that is not guaranteed, which can mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi yaha par seat bhi hai campus mein na. Kyunki almost saari seatein close ho chuki hai yaha par.
incidents.translation: There are still seats available in the campus. Because almost all seats have been closed here.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by claiming that almost all seats are closed while simultaneously stating that there are still seats available, which can mislead the student into thinking they must act quickly without verifying the actual situation.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to uske basis par aapko yaha par direct admission mil jayega.
incidents.translation: Based on that, you will get direct admission here.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student is guaranteed direct admission based on the registration form, which is misleading as admission processes typically involve more criteria.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and guarantee admission, which are clear cases of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: wahan par scholarship ka exam hota hai CGCUET ka to uske basis par aapko scholarship mil jayegi.
incidents.translation: There is a scholarship exam at CGC, CGCUET, based on which you will get a scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student will receive a scholarship based on an exam without clarifying the conditions or competitiveness of this scholarship, which can mislead the student about the financial feasibility.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To main to aapko yahi bolungi ki aap Kingston ka apply kar lo.
incidents.translation: So I will tell you to apply for Kingston.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pushing the student to apply to Kingston without addressing the student's budget concerns or ensuring it aligns with their interests, which could lead to a mismatch.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed tendencies of misselling by promoting scholarships without clear conditions and pushing a college that may not fit the student's budget.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki abhi jo hai KIIT ka abhi jo application form na close hone wale hai.
incidents.translation: because the KIIT application form is about to close now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies an urgent deadline for the application form without providing evidence, which can pressure the student to apply hastily.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding the application deadline for KIIT, which could mislead the student into applying without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh wahan par main uska Parul ka aapko bhej dungi registration link and fir aap Amity ka kar lena.
incidents.translation: So I will send you the registration link for Parul and then you can do Amity.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the last date for Parul University is today, implying that the student must act immediately without providing clear evidence of this deadline.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Wahan par unko itna criteria nahi hai unka strict ki aapko JEE mein so itna hona hi chahiye. Wahan par first come first serve ke basis pe rehta hai jiski wajah se ab unka seats almost full ho gaya and aaj last date hai.
incidents.translation: They do not have strict criteria that you must have this much in JEE. It is first come first serve, and because of that, their seats are almost full and today is the last date.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the seats are almost full and that today is the last date for registration, which may not be true, creating unnecessary pressure on the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor applied pressure by fabricating urgency regarding the registration deadlines for Parul University, which could mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To at least aapko CET ke basis pe to karna hi hai aapko. To uske liye hum wait kar sakte hai. But abhi kuch jo options hai jinki admission ya application close ho jayengi 18-19 tak, is week mein close ho jayengi most of the universities ki.
incidents.translation: So at least you have to do it based on CET. For that, we can wait. But right now, some options whose admissions or applications will close by 18-19, most of the universities will close this week.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that many universities will close their admissions soon, which may not be true or verified, pressuring the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan haan, ho jayega aapka. Direct 12th through. Bilkul ho jayega.
incidents.translation: Yes, yes, it will happen for you. Direct through 12th. It will definitely happen.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission based on the student's 12th grade, which is misleading as admission is not guaranteed and depends on various factors.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited clear instances of misselling by creating false urgency and overpromising admission outcomes, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: NIET ka to fee structure bhi kam hai. 4,12,000 hai only four years ka. Aur inke applications amount bhi abhi free of cost hai NIET mein.
incidents.translation: NIET's fee structure is also lower. It's only 4,12,000 for four years. And their application amount is currently free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that cost is the only factor to consider.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap jab applications aap form bharte hain na to application number generate hota hai. To jab aap university mein jaate hain agar aapke paas application number generate rehta hai to convenience hota hai baat karne mein.
incidents.translation: When you fill out the application forms, an application number is generated. When you go to the university, having the application number makes it convenient to talk.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying immediately is crucial for convenience, which creates a sense of urgency that may not be justified.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the free application as a primary motivator and created a sense of urgency around applying, which can mislead the student regarding the importance of immediate action.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aap one week lenge ya fir 10 din 15 din lagega to fir seats milne bahut hi tough ho jaayega.
incidents.translation: if you take one week or 10-15 days, then it will be very tough to get seats.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that waiting too long will significantly reduce the chances of securing a seat, which may not be substantiated by actual seat availability data.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aap soch rahe hain ki jaake apply karenge to wo thoda difficult hota hai. Wahan pe jaake apply karna. Chance bahut kam ho jaata hai.
incidents.translation: you are thinking of applying later, but that becomes a bit difficult. Applying there, the chances become very low.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying later will drastically reduce the chances of admission, which may not be accurate and is intended to pressure the student into applying sooner.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employs pressure tactics by exaggerating the urgency of applying to KCC, which could mislead the student regarding their actual chances of admission.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho B.Tech computer science me jaise ki aap pehle kisi bhi university me jaate ho na to aap apply karte ho to aapko application form fees rehti hai kuch 1500 rupees, 2000 rupees depends upon the university, right? NIET ki bhi around 1500 rupees ke kareeb fees hai available. But the thing is ki is campus me aapko ek benefit ye mil raha hai ki abhi na iske starting ke jo kuch 1500 to 2000 application hai usme inki fees waived off hai.
incidents.translation: Look, when you go to any university for B.Tech in computer science, you have to pay an application form fee of around 1500 rupees, 2000 rupees depending on the university, right? NIET also has fees around 1500 rupees. But the thing is, you are getting a benefit that currently, for these starting applications, the fees are waived off.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fee as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that this is a significant advantage without discussing the actual merits of the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To main ek baar check kar leta hu portal pe. Agar NIET ka application form apply ho raha hai fees waived off ke saath. To main suggest karta hu ki aap on call bhi apply kara lo abhi.
incidents.translation: So let me check the portal. If the NIET application form is available with the fees waived off. I suggest that you apply on call right now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting the student apply immediately based on the waived fees, which may not be a genuine time-sensitive offer.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the waived application fee and urgency to push the student towards applying for NIET, which can be seen as manipulative selling tactics.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To bacche kya karte hain us Bangalore wale offer letter ko show karte hain ki maine already Bangalore mein apply kiya tha, theek hai? Lekin mujhe wahan se 20% scholarship mil rahi hai aur aap sirf 15 de rahe hain.
incidents.translation: So what students do is they show the offer letter from Bangalore that I had already applied there, right? But I am getting a 20% scholarship from there and you are only giving 15.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that students can manipulate scholarship offers by leveraging offers from other campuses, which can mislead students into thinking they can guarantee a better deal, thus creating unrealistic expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To agar aap aisa karna chahte hain, to aap chheh campus aur hain jinke liye matlab aap free mein fill kar sakte hain.
incidents.translation: So if you want to do that, there are six other campuses for which you can fill for free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free' aspect of applications as a primary reason to apply, rather than focusing on the suitability of the colleges or courses, which can mislead the student about the importance of fit versus cost.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's suggestions regarding manipulating scholarship offers and the emphasis on free applications indicate clear cases of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aaj ke liye uska application fees bhi wave off hai.
incidents.translation: So today, the application fee is also waived off.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fee as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking this is a unique opportunity rather than a standard practice.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To ek mein to chalo aapka saving ho gaya, hai na?
incidents.translation: So in one, you have saved, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying to Shiksha Anusandhan is a guaranteed saving without discussing the actual admission criteria or competition, which can mislead the student about the ease of admission.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the waived application fee and the implication of guaranteed savings could mislead the student regarding the application process and admission likelihood.

incidents.counsellor_quote: UEM ka waise form free hai to in case aise aap apply karke dekhna chahti ho to kar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: The form for UEM is free, so in case you want to apply, you can do so.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan inka form free hai I think, ek baar main check kar leti hu.
incidents.translation: Yes, their form is free, I think, let me check once.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the free application is a strong incentive, which may mislead the student into thinking that applying is a guaranteed step towards admission.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application for UEM as a primary reason to apply raises concerns about potential misselling, as it downplays the importance of the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan aapne isko jaldi kar lo kyunki do minute mein na fir expire ho jayega.
incidents.translation: Yes, you should do this quickly because it will expire in two minutes.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the opportunity will expire in a very short time, which may not be true and pressures the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the urgency to complete the application is misleading and creates unnecessary pressure on the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko koi payment nahi karni padegi. Kyunki aapne Shiksha se already jaise NIIT ke liye apply kiya tha to agar aap Amity fill karna chahte ho aapke liye offer rahega seven campuses pe.
incidents.translation: You won't have to make any payment. Since you have already applied for NIIT through Shiksha, if you want to fill for Amity, you will have an offer for seven campuses.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free' application process as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of evaluating the college's fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application process could mislead the student into prioritizing cost over the suitability of the colleges discussed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi aap, dekho aapne abhi tak kiya nahi hai na, agar aap nahi karoge to wo phir fill kar lenge na.
incidents.translation: No, look, you haven't done it yet, if you don't do it, then they will fill it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pressuring the student by implying that if they don't act quickly, another student will take their spot, creating a false sense of urgency.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor applied pressure to the student to complete the application quickly, which is misleading and manipulative.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki 4 baje college close ho jayega isliye woh mujhe update karna rehta hai.
incidents.translation: because the college will close at 4 PM, that's why I need to update you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the college will close soon, which may pressure the student to act quickly without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki usme direct admission hai na to zyada seat nahi rehti hai isliye main aapse bol rahi hoon.
incidents.translation: because there is direct admission, there aren't many seats left, that's why I'm telling you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests a fabricated scarcity of seats to pressure the student into making a quick decision, which is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency tactics that could mislead the student into making a rushed decision regarding the application.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Achha to ye to mere khayal se SRMU University to pan India mein bahut jagah hai na jaise. But aap iske liye main batati hu aap, iske liye apply karo kyunki iske liye na mere paas coupon code hai.
incidents.translation: Oh, I think SRMU University is available in many places across India. But I am telling you to apply for this because I have a coupon code for it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is primarily pushing the student to apply based on the availability of a coupon code, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student's needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To wo coupon code aap upload karoge na to usme aapko discount mil jayega to koi fees nahi lagega usme application fees.
incidents.translation: So if you upload that coupon code, you will get a discount, and there will be no application fees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that the application fee can be waived with a coupon code, which may mislead the student into thinking that applying is entirely free without considering the actual costs involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the availability of a coupon code as a primary reason to encourage application, which can be seen as a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka na application free of cost hai.
incidents.translation: The application for Amity is free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that the application is free of cost without clarifying that it is actually a waived fee, which can mislead the student into thinking there are no costs involved at all.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Maine coupon code already apply kar diya hai to 1100 rupaye ka application form hai but free of cost me hi apply ho jayega.
incidents.translation: I have already applied the coupon code, so the application form of 1100 rupees will be applied free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the application fee is waived without proper context, which can mislead the student into thinking that there are no fees at all.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the application fee being free, which could confuse the student regarding actual costs associated with the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To agar isme 50% bhi scholarship aapko milti hai to that means ki aapki 50% fees less ho rahi hai.
incidents.translation: So if you get even 50% scholarship in this, it means your fees will be reduced by 50%.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that a 50% scholarship is guaranteed, which may not be the case, thus misleading the student about the financial implications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki mere paas hai last coupon code.
incidents.translation: Because I have the last coupon code.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the coupon code is the last one available, which may not be true, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that misled the student regarding scholarship availability and created a false sense of urgency around the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh dekho, university ka deadline hai. Unke paas limited seats hain. Haan, unke paas limited seats hain. Kyunki Chandigarh University ek brand university hai.
incidents.translation: So look, the university has a deadline. They have limited seats. Yes, they do have limited seats. Because Chandigarh University is a brand university.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency regarding limited seats at Chandigarh University without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency about limited seats at Chandigarh University, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan, yahan pe aapka chance hai ki aapka ho jaayega.
incidents.translation: Yes, here you have a chance that you will get in.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that the student will definitely get admission based on their current scores, which is misleading as it does not account for the actual admission criteria.

incidents.counsellor_quote: So iska apply karte raho aur baaki Amity ka jo hai main check kar, bharte reh raha hoon, theek hai? Aur iska jo application fees hai wo like free of cost hai, iska aapko like paisa nahi lagega.
incidents.translation: So keep applying for this and I will check for Amity, keep filling it. And the application fees for this is free of cost, you won't have to pay any money.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to encourage the student to apply, rather than focusing on the college's merits or fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the likelihood of admission and emphasized the free application as a key reason to apply, which is not appropriate.

incidents.counsellor_quote: phase one mein na only 2% seat hai
incidents.translation: In phase one, there are only 2% seats available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by claiming that only 2% of seats are available without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's claim about the limited availability of seats is misleading and serves to pressure the student into applying quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to mere paas coupon code hai aap kar lena inme bhi fill to free of cost mein apply ho jayega koi fees nahi lagegi main coupon code de dungi aapko.
incidents.translation: I have a coupon code for you, so you can apply free of cost without any fees; I will give you the coupon code.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the 'free of cost' aspect as the primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits, which can mislead the student into thinking that the application is without any real considerations.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on a 'free application' through a coupon code raises concerns about the motivations behind the application process, indicating potential misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Shaam tak coupon code nahi rahega na mere paas.
incidents.translation: The coupon code won't be with me by evening.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the coupon code will expire soon, which may not be true, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the urgency of the coupon code creates pressure on the student to apply quickly, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Main aapko coupon code share kar rahi hu to Amity mein to aap sab mein hi form fill kar lo. Kyunki isme main ek coupon code de dungi to free of cost mein hi fill ho jayega.
incidents.translation: I am sharing a coupon code for Amity, so you can fill the form for all of them. Because I will give you a coupon code, it will be filled free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free application as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the suitability of the college or course for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application process could mislead the student into prioritizing this over a proper evaluation of their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But yeh hai ki BCA ke liye na yeh log woh provide karte hain scholarship wagera. Toh bas isliye main aapko chah rahi thi ho jaaye ki at least form mein toh koi payment lagega nahi, free hai form toh, registration charges zero hai.
incidents.translation: But for BCA, they provide scholarships. So that's why I wanted you to at least apply because there won't be any payment for the form, it's free, the registration charges are zero.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing that the application is free as the primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit for the student's needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur agar uske baad aapko lagta hai ki tab bhi fees agar zyada hai, scholarship milne ke baad, toh phir aap mana kar sakte ho, koi issue hi nahi hai.
incidents.translation: And if after that you feel that the fees are still high, after getting the scholarship, then you can refuse, there is no issue.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student can easily back out after applying, which may mislead the student into thinking that applying is a low-stakes decision without fully understanding the implications.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the free application as a primary selling point and suggested that the student could easily back out after applying, which can be misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki kal last date hai CGC ki, to aaj hi aapko apply karna padega, 14 April is the last date.
incidents.translation: Because tomorrow is the last date for CGC, so you have to apply today, 14 April is the last date.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the last date is tomorrow without providing evidence or context, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the urgency of the application deadline for CGC Mohali is misleading and could pressure the student into applying without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap apply karo aur ye dekho ki unke interviews call kaise aa rahe hain, unke internal cheezein janne ki koshish karo uske baad aap admission lena hai nahi lena is a secondary thing.
incidents.translation: You apply and see how their interview calls are coming, try to know their internal things, after that whether you want to take admission or not is a secondary thing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes applying to the university primarily to explore and practice interviews without clearly addressing the student's concerns about the university's fit, which can be seen as a nudge to apply without sufficient justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Main try karti hun ki main kuch aisi cheezein aapko bheju jisse aap convince ho jao kyunki SRM... it would not going to waste.
incidents.translation: I will try to send you some things that will convince you because SRM... it would not going to waste.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student will not waste their investment in SRM without addressing the student's specific doubts, implying a guarantee of value that may not be substantiated.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included nudging the student towards applying without adequately addressing their concerns, which can be seen as a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: SRM mein jo early admission benefits tha wo ab close ho raha hai.
incidents.translation: The early admission benefits in SRM are now closing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the early admission benefits are about to close, creating a sense of urgency without providing clear evidence or details about the actual deadline, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To wo ek positive effect hota hai. Iski wajah se aap directly apply karo to zyada better hai.
incidents.translation: This has a positive effect. Because of this, applying directly is better.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying directly will significantly increase the chances of admission without substantiating this claim, which could mislead the student about their actual chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise the likelihood of admission based on the method of application, which can pressure the student into a decision without adequate information.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh agar aapke confusion mind mein aa raha hai placements record ko leke, to usko option mein leke chalo, confirm karke mat chalo mind mein.
incidents.translation: So if you have any confusion in your mind regarding the placement record, keep it as an option, don't go with it in your mind.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pushing SRM by suggesting that the student should consider it as an option due to confusion about placements, which could be seen as a pressure tactic without addressing the student's preferences.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh at least ek private university aap leke chalo kyunki inka fee structure aapne jahan apply kiya usse bhi kam hai, Amity se bhi kam hai.
incidents.translation: So at least take one private university because their fee structure is lower than where you have applied, even lower than Amity.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the fee structure of SRM as a reason to choose it, which could mislead the student into thinking it is a better option without providing sufficient context or comparison.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan aur SRM ka aaj early admissions ka last date hai. Early admissions mein karne pe confirmation ke chances zyada hote hain aur scholarship benefits ke milne ka chances bhi higher hote hain.
incidents.translation: And today is the last date for early admissions for SRM. By applying for early admissions, the chances of confirmation are higher and the chances of getting scholarship benefits are also higher.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for early admissions, which may not be accurate or could be exaggerated to pressure the student into making a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed several instances of pushing SRM with urgency and potentially misleading claims about placement records and fee structures, which could pressure the student into a decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi admission bhi means application bhi close ho rahe hai.
incidents.translation: Currently, admissions and applications are closing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency regarding the application process without providing evidence of an actual deadline, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to main aapko suggest karungi ki fees ke according GL Bajaj aapke liye best rahega.
incidents.translation: So I would suggest that according to the fees, GL Bajaj would be the best for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents GL Bajaj as the best option without acknowledging the student's specific needs or preferences, which could mislead the student into believing it is the only viable choice.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited signs of misselling by creating a false sense of urgency and overpromising the suitability of GL Bajaj without considering the student's individual profile.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki abhi aapka free of cost ka application fees hai, koi fees nahi lag rahi hai aapki.
incidents.translation: because right now your application fee is free of cost, there is no fee for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free of cost' application fee as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the fit of the college or course for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application fee could be seen as a mild form of misselling, as it may distract from more relevant factors for the student's decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: phase one mein agar bachche apply karte hain na to usme minimum fifty to sixty percent scholarship to mil jaati hai up to.
incidents.translation: In phase one, if students apply, they can get a minimum of fifty to sixty percent scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that students will receive a significant scholarship without clarifying that this is contingent on performance in the entrance exam, which could mislead the student into thinking they are guaranteed this amount.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki aaj na iska phase one close ho jaayega.
incidents.translation: Because today is the last day for phase one.
