misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and promise significant scholarships without clear conditions, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aise toh aap form hi nahi fill paoge. Aise toh kyunki is university mein bahut accha campus hai, aapke side mein hi baitha hua hai yeh campus.
incidents.translation: Otherwise, you won't be able to fill the form. Because this university has a very good campus, it is right near your area.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Chhodho mat iska application form.
incidents.translation: Don't leave this application form.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics to encourage the student to apply for Chandigarh University, which could be considered manipulative and misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aise kaafi colleges aise bhi hain jo free registration provide kar rahe hain. To jaise agar abhi tak aapne kuch bhi sure nahi hai.
incidents.translation: There are many colleges that provide free registration. So if you are not sure about anything yet.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan, sirf Maharashtra mein nahi iska campus, pan India bahut saari jagah par hai.
incidents.translation: Yes, it's not just in Maharashtra, there are many places across India.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the college has a wide reach and validity, which could mislead the student regarding the college's actual reputation and recognition across different regions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on free registration and broad claims about the college's reach could mislead the student regarding the importance of fit and the actual standing of the institution.

incidents.counsellor_quote: toh jahan par abhi benefit hai, you know aapko bhi nahi pata ki do teen mahine baad kya pata kuch Amity ko lekar hi plans banne lage tab 1100 rupaye doge us form ko bharne ke liye aur abhi form free hai aapka.
incidents.translation: So where there is currently a benefit, you never know what plans might come up regarding Amity in two or three months, then you will have to pay 1100 rupees for filling that form, and right now the form is free for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is primarily pushing the free registration for Amity as a reason to apply, without providing substantial reasons related to the student's profile or interests, which is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor emphasized the free application as a primary reason to apply to Amity, which is a clear case of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aaj last date hai CGC ka.
incidents.translation: Today is the last date for CGC.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that today is the last date for CGC admissions without providing any evidence, creating a sense of urgency that may not be true.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a false sense of urgency regarding the application deadline, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Chandigarh ka to kal extend ho jayega lekin LPU ka aaj last date hai.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity ka jo campus hai na wo Bangalore me bhi hai aur Mumbai me bhi hai... uski bhi fees jo hai wo waived off aa rahi hai.
incidents.translation: Amity's campus is also in Bangalore and Mumbai... and its fees are being waived off.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used inflated urgency regarding deadlines and fee waivers, which could mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi iska jo apply application form hai uska fees waived off hai, lag nahi raha hai.
incidents.translation: Right now, the application form for this is waived off, it doesn't cost anything.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the waived application fee as a significant incentive to push for an application, which can be seen as a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko 20% mil rahi hai, 30% mil rahi hai, 40% mil rahi hai, benefit to aapka hi hai na.
incidents.translation: You are getting 20%, 30%, 40%, the benefit is yours, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student can receive significant scholarships based on entrance test performance, which may not be guaranteed and could mislead the student into thinking they will definitely receive a substantial discount.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Mam yeh last application form na Chandigarh ka 16 tarikh tak hi open hai.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about scholarship availability and application deadlines create a misleading sense of urgency and promise benefits that may not be guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: NIIT campus bahut better university hai, aap uska bhi plan kar sakte ho. Us campus ke andar agar aap admission karwate ho to mam is campus me na computer science ke andar bachho ka 50 lakhs tak ka placement ho rakha hai.
incidents.translation: The NIIT campus is a much better university, you can also plan for it. If you get admission in this campus, then there is a placement of up to 50 lakhs for students in computer science.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim of '50 lakhs' placement is exaggerated and lacks verification, which misleads the student about the actual placement potential.

incidents.counsellor_quote: basically for today itself Saturday hai so NIIT ka jo application form ka fees hai wo waived off hai completely.
incidents.translation: Basically, since today is Saturday, the application fee for NIIT is completely waived off.
incidents.why_misselling: While the fee waiver is true, the emphasis on applying immediately due to the fee waiver can pressure the student to make a decision without considering all options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made exaggerated claims about placement opportunities at NIIT and used the free application fee as a nudge to encourage immediate application, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to admission confirm like agar maan lo aap earlier stage me application form apply karte hain to there is a like 80% chances ki seats confirmation aa jaati hain
incidents.translation: So for admission confirmation, if you apply earlier, there is like an 80% chance that seat confirmation happens.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying a high likelihood of admission based on early application, which can be misleading as it suggests a guarantee that may not exist.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki us time jo seats availability zyada hoti hai na to seat confirmation ke chances bhi zyada ho jaate hain
incidents.translation: Because at that time, the availability of seats is more, so the chances of seat confirmation also increase.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates a sense of urgency and suggests that seats will be more available at a certain time, which may not be accurate or verifiable.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about high chances of admission and seat availability that could mislead the student into thinking there are guaranteed outcomes based on early application.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 1 baje tak aap mujhe confirm karo kyunki 4 baje tak report submit karni hoti hai early admissions ka.
incidents.translation: Please confirm by 1 PM because I have to submit the report for early admissions by 4 PM.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's insistence on a tight deadline for confirmation could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply quickly without fully considering their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi aisa nahi hai. Application to aap pehle kar sakte ho. Basically application se ye benefit milta hai aapko ki university na aapko first come first serve ke basis pe kaam karti hai.
incidents.translation: No, that's not the case. You can apply earlier. Basically, applying gives you the benefit that the university works on a first come first serve basis.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying early significantly increases admission chances due to a first-come-first-serve policy, which may not be a guaranteed or universally applicable practice.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To jaise suppose aap application fill karte ho to aapke chances zyada ho jaate hain admission ke.
incidents.translation: So, for example, if you fill out the application, your chances of admission increase.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that simply filling out the application will increase the student's chances of admission, which could mislead the student into thinking that applying guarantees admission.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about the benefits of early application and increased chances of admission that could mislead the student regarding the actual admission process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi, problem to nahi aayegi lekin yehi hai na ke jo ten plus two ke basis pe hai na, haan ji, ek iska, ek second.
incidents.translation: No, there won't be a problem, but the thing is that for those based on ten plus two, yes, one of these, one second.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the student needs to act quickly due to potential issues with the number of applications, which may not be a factual representation of the situation.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Fir bhi kyunki kya hai dekho chhe baje working close ho jayegi aur agar aap uske baad karoge na to fir jo form hai aapka, wo fir main kal forward kar paunga aage.
incidents.translation: Still, because what happens is that at six o'clock the working will close, and if you do it after that, then the form of yours, I will be able to forward it tomorrow.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the university will close at a specific time, which may not be true, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency tactics that could mislead the student into thinking they need to act quickly to secure their admission, which is a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki iski seats to bahut jaldi fill ho jayengi aapki.
incidents.translation: Because the seats will fill up very quickly.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that seats will fill up quickly without providing evidence or context, which may pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapko koi cheez aapke budget mein mil rahi hai, placement aapko achha mil raha hai, saari cheezein aapko achhi mil rahi hai to aapko form fill to kar hi lena chahiye.
incidents.translation: If you are getting something within your budget, good placements, and everything is good for you, then you should definitely fill the form.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor pressures the student to fill out the application by emphasizing the benefits without allowing the student to fully consider their options, which can lead to a rushed decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited pressure tactics to encourage the student to apply quickly, which constitutes a form of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapka JEE ka result aa gaya, aapka VIT ka exam hone wala hai, hai na KIIT ka bhi ek baar aap check karna uska bhi examination aane wala hoga.
incidents.translation: Your JEE result has come, your VIT exam is about to happen, and you should also check for KIIT's examination coming up.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the student must act quickly due to upcoming exams and limited seat availability, which may not be substantiated.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapka achi category ka placements hain usme, highest placements agar aap fees se comparison karoge to fees uski 4 lakh bhi nahi hai aur jo highest placement hai wo around 49 lakhs ka hai.
incidents.translation: It has good category placements, if you compare the highest placements with the fees, the fees are not even 4 lakhs and the highest placement is around 49 lakhs.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests an unrealistic return on investment (ROI) by comparing the highest placement to the fees without clarifying that such placements are not guaranteed for all students.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency and overpromised on placement outcomes, which could mislead the student regarding their options and expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapka yahan pe 1400 rupees ka application form lagta hai yahan pe. ... agar aap apna coupon code apply karte hain na usme ki aapka 1400 rupees ka hai na matlab complete 100% waived off ho jaata hai woh fees aapka.
incidents.translation: You have a 1400 rupees application form here. ... if you apply your coupon code, then your 1400 rupees will be completely waived off.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's merits or fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aur B.Tech ka CS branch tha aapka kaunsa tha? ... matlab kaafi achha jaata tha like 15 inki ranking thi.
incidents.translation: And what was your B.Tech CS branch? ... it was quite good, like they had a ranking of 15.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the free application as a primary selling point and made claims about the college's ranking that could mislead the student regarding its quality.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi even agar aap application bhi apply karte ho na to uska application fee jo hai na wo bhi wave off hai, free of cost hai abhi application fee SOA ka.
incidents.translation: Even if you apply for the application now, the application fee is waived off, it is free of cost for SOA.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free application fee as a primary reason to apply, rather than focusing on the college's merits or fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: SOA is better aur abhi even agar aap application bhi apply karte ho na to uska application fee jo hai na wo bhi wave off hai, free of cost hai abhi application fee SOA ka.
incidents.translation: SOA is better and even if you apply for the application now, the application fee is waived off, it is free of cost for SOA.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that applying to SOA is a guaranteed good choice without adequately discussing the student's specific needs or other options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the free application fee as a primary selling point for SOA University, which could mislead the student into thinking it is the best option without considering their specific needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap already bahut late ho. Like registration has started from October itself.
incidents.translation: You are already very late. Like registration has started from October itself.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student is late for registration, creating a sense of urgency without clear evidence that this is the case, potentially pressuring the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi humne kuch colleges suggest kar decide karne hain jiske through hume jaana hai to usme aap application yehi maan ke chalo ki last date aaj kal mein hi hai.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics regarding application deadlines, which could mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan haan NAAC approval hai tabhi bataya hai.
incidents.translation: Yes, yes, it has NAAC approval, that's why I mentioned it.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap mujhe apni basic details wagera provide karenge na to main aapke behalf pe apply bhi kar dungi.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of the application process and admission requirements could mislead the student regarding the ease of admission and the significance of the application fee.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aur woh free like without any cost apply ho raha hai to maine kaha aap dekh lo jaise aapko.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar apply karna hai to aap aaj ke date mein apply kar lo, to main waise to form iska 1500 ka hai, agar aap aaj ke date mein apply karte ho to aapko sirf aur sirf 299 rupees pay karna hoga.

incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents a fabricated urgency by stating that only 2.5% slots are available without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding the availability of slots, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Jaise Amity ka aapka Raipur ka free hai, Mohali ka free hai, Bangalore ka free hai, Hyderabad ka free hua hai.
incidents.translation: Like Amity's Raipur is free, Mohali is free, Bangalore is free, Hyderabad has become free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that multiple branches of Amity are free, suggesting that the opportunity may not last, which can pressure the student to act quickly without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Jaise Amity ke mostly branches ka aaj free hua hai but I think aadhe ek ghante ke liye free hai ye.
incidents.translation: Like most branches of Amity have become free today but I think this is free for only half an hour.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a fabricated sense of urgency by suggesting that the free application is only available for a limited time, which may not be true, pressuring the student to apply immediately.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency tactics regarding free applications for Amity University, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki aaj uska na early bird ka last date hai isliye main pooch rahi thi.
incidents.translation: Because today is the last date for the early bird, that's why I was asking.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for early bird applications without providing evidence that this is true, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapne koi direct admission nahi, matlab koi entrance exam nahi diya hai to aap yahan pe direct admission nahi le sakte ho.
incidents.translation: If you haven't given any entrance exam, then you cannot take direct admission here.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement implies that admission is guaranteed only if the student has taken an entrance exam, which may not be the case, thus misleading the student about their options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited misleading urgency regarding application deadlines and made claims about admission processes that could mislead the student about their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap abhi karte ho toh aapka free of cost main aapka karwa dungi.
incidents.translation: If you apply now, I will apply for you free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free of cost as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the application is only valuable because it is free, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application process could mislead the student regarding the value of the college choice.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Bahut din tak yeh open nahi rahega, agar aapke paas time hai toh main aaj kar deti hoon
incidents.translation: It won't be open for many days, if you have time then I can do it today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the application will not be available for long without providing any evidence or specific deadline, which can pressure the student into acting quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the application not being open for long creates unnecessary pressure on the student to apply immediately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aaj nahi hoga na toh phir yeh pending mein chala jayega, phir KIIT ke liye nahi apply kar payenge aap.
incidents.translation: If it doesn't happen today, then it will go pending, and you won't be able to apply for KIIT.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the application will be lost if not completed today, which may not be true.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's insistence on immediate action creates pressure on the student, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan PDPU mein admissions ho jaata hai.
incidents.translation: Yes, admissions happen in PDPU.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that admissions are guaranteed at PDPU without acknowledging the student's actual eligibility or the competitive nature of the admissions process, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To agar let's suppose thoda bahut agar kam score bhi aata hai na theek hai to na university apne taraf se try to karti hai.
incidents.translation: So if, let's suppose, a little lower score comes, then the university tries on its own.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the university will make efforts to admit students even with lower scores, which can create unrealistic expectations about the admissions process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several statements that could mislead the student into believing that admission to certain colleges is more assured than it realistically is, which constitutes misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Uska through registration close hone wala hai, like aapka is mahine mid of April se leke 28 of April ke beech mein aapka exam padega.
incidents.translation: The registration is going to close, like your exam will be between mid of April to 28 of April.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency regarding the registration deadline without providing clear evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur registration aapka 1000 rupees ka hai aur isi aapke bhai ke number se aap karenge to 200 rupees ka cashback milega.
incidents.translation: And the registration is 1000 rupees, and if you do it with your brother's number, you will get a cashback of 200 rupees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor mentions the registration fee and cashback but does not provide a comprehensive overview of the total costs involved, which could mislead the student about the financial commitment required.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a sense of urgency and gloss over the total fees involved, which can mislead the student regarding the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Because kai baar fir ye free nahi hote hain.
incidents.translation: Because sometimes later they are not free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the opportunity to apply for UEM may not remain free in the future, creating a sense of urgency that is not substantiated.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To isse better hai aap four to five colleges aur option mein leke chaliye.
incidents.translation: So it's better to take four to five more colleges as options.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying to multiple colleges is a guaranteed way to secure admission, which may mislead the student into thinking that admission is assured if they apply to more colleges.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise the likelihood of admission, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To iska free of cost actually weekend hai na to isme hamare paas coupon aata hai to wo free of cost coupon aap use kar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: Actually, this is free of cost because it's the weekend, so we have a coupon available that you can use for free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking the college is a better option solely due to the waived fee, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student's needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To main fir fill kar leta hu fir ise.
incidents.translation: So I will fill this for you now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the student should fill the form immediately to take advantage of the coupon, which may not be necessary and pressures the student into a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included pushing for immediate application based on a free coupon and creating a sense of urgency, which can be seen as manipulative tactics rather than providing balanced guidance.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To try karo jald se jald apply karna start kar do kyunki BPT jo specialization hai na wo har ek college mein milega nahi aur agar mil jaayega to usme jo number of seats rahengi na 30 se zyada bahut kam colleges aise hote hain, 10 mein se do ya teen college aise milenge jisme 60 seats milengi.
incidents.translation: So try to start applying as soon as possible because the BPT specialization is not available in every college, and if it is available, the number of seats will be more than 30 in very few colleges, maybe two or three out of ten will have 60 seats.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that BPT specializations are scarce and that seats are limited without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about the scarcity of BPT seats and the urgency to apply can mislead the student into feeling pressured to act quickly without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar abhi nahi karoge baad mein kya hoga na, aisa nahi hai ki aap baad mein nahi kar sakte ho. Baad mein bhi kar sakte ho but uss time pe problem yeh aata hai na ki agar abhi is time pe first come first basis pe agar aapko. 30-40% tak scholarship mil sakti hai na, aap baad mein apply karogi to yehi scholarship aapki 10 se 15% ho jaati hai.
incidents.translation: If you don't do it now, what will happen later, it's not that you can't do it later. You can apply later, but the problem is that if you apply now, you can get a scholarship of 30-40%, but if you apply later, that scholarship will drop to 10-15%.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that delaying the application will significantly reduce the scholarship amount, which may not be a guaranteed outcome and could mislead the student into applying sooner than they are ready.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of scholarship percentages creates pressure on the student to apply immediately, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh agar aap bharte ho toh isse aapko fayda pata hai kya hoga? Aapke paas jaise saare campus ke offer letter honge, aapke paas zyada offer letter honge toh ek toh confidence boost up hoga, aapka interview ka bhi practice hoga, in addition to that aapko Amity mein agar admission lena hai toh aapke paas na best choices honge Amity ke campus ke.
incidents.translation: So if you fill it, what benefit will you get? You will have all the campus offer letters, you will have more offer letters, so it will boost your confidence, you will also practice for interviews, and in addition to that, if you want to take admission in Amity, you will have the best choices among Amity's campuses.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is primarily pushing the idea of applying to multiple campuses of Amity University by suggesting that it will boost the student's confidence and provide them with more options, which can be seen as a manipulative nudge rather than focusing on the student's specific needs or interests.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh agar aapke paas kisi campus mein 10%, 20%, 30%, 40% tak scholarship hogi.
incidents.translation: So if you have a scholarship of 10%, 20%, 30%, or 40% at any campus.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that having multiple applications will lead to guaranteed scholarships, which is misleading as it conditions the scholarships on enrolling first and does not clarify the actual chances of receiving such offers.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included pushing for applications to multiple campuses of Amity University with vague promises of benefits and scholarships, which can be seen as manipulative and misleading.

incidents.college_or_course: Ambika Institute of Management and Technology
incidents.counsellor_quote: Are abhi bahut saare colleges ki last date chal rahi hai na.
incidents.translation: Right now, many colleges have their last dates ongoing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency regarding application deadlines without providing specific details or evidence, which may pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap management quota se jaoge to aapko wahi ek saal ki fees 2,60,000 padegi.
incidents.translation: If you go through management quota, your annual fees will be 2,60,000.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that management quota admission is a viable option without clarifying the actual chances of securing a seat, which can mislead the student into believing that admission is guaranteed if they pay the higher fees.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited signs of pushing for urgency and overpromising admission through management quota, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Achha theek hai. Toh aap mujhe bata dena agar aapko mera help chahiye hoga toh main aapko call par help le lungi, aap sync kar sakte ho taaki kuch bhi issues aayega main aapko hand to hand clear kar dungi. Kyunki uska bhi free hai and woh next sirf 24 hours ke liye applicable hai, theek hai?
incidents.translation: Alright, okay. So let me know if you need my help, I can assist you over the call, you can sync so that if any issues arise, I will clear them for you hand to hand. Because it is also free and this is only applicable for the next 24 hours, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the urgency of a 'free' registration for Amity University, suggesting that the student should act quickly without providing substantial reasons for why this college is a good fit, which can be seen as manipulative.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh agar aapka 93 above aa jaata hai na 12th mein, so you will be eligible for 100% scholarship which your first year ke liye applicable hoga.
incidents.translation: So if you score above 93 in 12th, you will be eligible for a 100% scholarship which will be applicable for your first year.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor promises a 100% scholarship based on a specific score, which may not be guaranteed and could mislead the student into thinking that admission is assured if they meet that score.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included pressure to register quickly for a college based on a free application offer and made promises regarding scholarships that could mislead the student about their admission prospects.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aapko just kya karna hai, apna interview wagera agar aap qualify kar paate ho to uske base pe aapko option mil jaata hai college ka.
incidents.translation: So all you have to do is, if you qualify for your interview, then based on that you will get an option for the college.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of the student's fit for the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to aap mujhe inform karna, I will give you a coupon code jismein aapka 70 to 80% application charges aapka off ho jayega uske liye bhi.
incidents.translation: So let me know, I will give you a coupon code that will give you a 70 to 80% discount on your application charges.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that applying now will lead to significant discounts, which may not be a genuine offer.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach includes pushing for applications based on free offers and discounts, which can mislead the student regarding the true value and urgency of the options presented.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar maan lo in case Amity mein bhi scholarship milta hai par wahi hai ki first come first serve dete hain.
incidents.translation: if you get a scholarship in Amity, but the thing is they give it on a first come first serve basis.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that a scholarship is available but conditions it on applying first, which can mislead the student into thinking they will receive a scholarship without clarifying the actual requirements or availability.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aap Amity ka agar free bhi hoga na to aap agar application pehle daaloge to hi aapke chances zyada honge aapke.
incidents.translation: if Amity is free, then if you apply first, your chances will be higher.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that applying quickly is the only way to secure a spot or scholarship, which may not be entirely accurate.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about scholarships and urgency in applying could mislead the student regarding their chances and the actual process involved.

incidents.counsellor_quote: So if you are interested, you can just share it to me and I will complete the application and I will share you the PDF. Okay?
incidents.translation: So if you are interested, you can just share it to me and I will complete the application and I will share you the PDF. Okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that there are no charges for the application process, which may mislead the student into thinking that applying is entirely free without acknowledging potential fees later in the process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: If you will perform well, they can provide you offer letter.
incidents.translation: If you perform well, they can provide you an offer letter.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a strong likelihood of receiving an offer letter based solely on performance in the interview, which can be misleading as admission is not guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims that could mislead the student regarding the application process and the certainty of admission, which constitutes misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki colleges ke jo application hai wo 13 se 15 me close hone wale hain
incidents.translation: because the college applications will close between the 13th and 15th
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating specific dates for application closures without verifying if those dates are accurate or applicable, which may pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about application deadlines appears to be misleading, as it may not reflect the actual situation, creating unnecessary pressure on the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To abhi fill kar du ya fir aap wait karenge?
incidents.translation: So should I fill it now or will you wait?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pushing the student to fill out the application for Amity by emphasizing that it is currently free, which could mislead the student into thinking that applying is the only option without considering their preferences.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab jitna late karenge to aapke paas option bhi nahi rahega, ye to abhi free hai. Future mein ho sakta hai na free ho.
incidents.translation: The longer you wait, the fewer options you will have; this is free right now. It may not be free in the future.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the opportunity to apply for free may not last, which is misleading if there is no evidence to support this claim.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor applied pressure by emphasizing the free application and creating a false sense of urgency, which could mislead the student into applying without fully considering their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap sochte reh jaoge to jitne best colleges ke options hain na wo hath se chale jayenge.
incidents.translation: If you keep thinking, the best college options will slip out of your hands.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency that if the student does not act quickly, they will lose out on the best college options, which may not be true.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap choose karo and like uske baad agar ye aapka like interest zone hai to interest zone ke liye aap kahin se certification course karte raho.
incidents.translation: So you choose and then if this is your interest zone, you should keep doing certification courses.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that choosing a BBA will guarantee the student a path aligned with their interests and future career, which may not be the case.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed signs of urgency and made promises regarding the alignment of the BBA course with the student's career goals, which could be misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Iski abhi application free of cost hai.
incidents.translation: Its application is currently free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the application is free, while it typically has a fee of 1500, which could mislead the student into thinking there are no costs involved.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To uske baad aapko offer letter aata hai ki like aapko itni scholarship offer ki ja rahi hai.
incidents.translation: After that, you will receive an offer letter stating the scholarship being offered to you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student will receive a scholarship offer after applying, which may not be guaranteed and could mislead the student into thinking they will definitely receive financial aid.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about the application process and scholarship offers could mislead the student regarding costs and guaranteed outcomes.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko koi application nahi form ka fees nahi dena hai, right? Aap low budget mein college dekh rahe ho, right? And aap thoda zyada spend karne ka nahi plan kar rahe ho to aap Amity Mumbai ko apply karke dekh sakte ho.
incidents.translation: You don't have to pay any application form fees, right? You are looking for a college within a low budget, right? And you are not planning to spend much, so you can consider applying to Amity Mumbai.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fee as a primary reason to apply, rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student's needs, which can mislead the student into thinking that the fee waiver is the most important factor.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application fee as a key reason to apply to Amity Mumbai indicates a potential case of misselling, as it downplays the importance of the college's suitability for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But inka abhi scholarship chal raha hai that is CUCET ka test, inka online test hota hai and uske base pe abhi 100% scholarship hai jo bhi aap score karoge.
incidents.translation: But currently they have a scholarship going on which is the CUCET test, it is an online test and based on that there is currently a 100% scholarship available depending on your score.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that students can achieve a 100% scholarship based on their test scores, which may not be guaranteed and could mislead the student into thinking they will receive significant financial aid without acknowledging the conditions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's claims about the scholarship at Chandigarh University could mislead the student regarding the likelihood of receiving such financial aid.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap chaho to iske liye apply kar sakte ho ek backup taiyar ho jayega uske liye aapko kuch invest bhi nahi karna hai zero rupees hai na ek tarike se free hai.
incidents.translation: So you can apply for this, it will prepare a backup for you, and you don't have to invest anything, it's zero rupees, in a way it's free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that applying is free as a primary reason to encourage the student to apply, rather than focusing on the college's fit or benefits.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To ek to budget mein hai accha college hai Noida mein hai placement bhi acche hain decent 6 7 lakh ka average mil raha hai matlab fees se zyada aapko return mil raha hai.
incidents.translation: So it's within budget, it's a good college in Noida, and the placements are good, with a decent average of 6 to 7 lakhs, meaning you are getting more return than the fees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student will definitely receive a good return on investment without substantiating the claims with specific data or context, which can mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To iska jo iska form hai theek hai aap chaho to iske liye apply kar sakte hain iska form free of cost hai abhi ke liye.
incidents.translation: So the form for this is available, you can apply for it, and the form is free of cost for now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the form is free for a limited time, which can pressure the student into making a quick decision without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that could mislead the student regarding the urgency and benefits of applying, as well as overpromising on the potential returns from the college investment.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aapko Kalinga mein apply karna hai because that is the only good college in Odisha for both the courses, toh aap apply kar lo aaj, right?
incidents.translation: if you want to apply to Kalinga because that is the only good college in Odisha for both the courses, then you should apply today, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that Kalinga is the 'only good college' and implies that the opportunity to apply may not be available later, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kal last date tha but inka na aaj ek din ke liye application form extend hua hai.
incidents.translation: yesterday was the last date, but today the application form has been extended for one day.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor fabricates a deadline extension to pressure the student into applying immediately, which is a manipulative tactic.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics and misleading claims about the urgency of applying to Kalinga, which constitutes clear misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapko Galgotia se call aaya hai to unhone khud aapko bataya hoga ki kya kehte hain unhone aapko khud bola hoga ki aap kar lo.
incidents.translation: If you have received a call from Galgotias, they must have told you themselves that you should go ahead.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the student must act quickly based on a call from the college, which may not reflect the actual situation or necessity.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Jitna jaldi ho sake haan Sahitya kal ko agar aap Galgotia mein nahi bhi jaate ho na to aap ye mat socho ki aapka bahut bada koi loss ho gaya hai.
incidents.translation: As soon as possible, yes Sahitya, even if you don't go to Galgotias tomorrow, don't think that you have suffered a huge loss.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates a sense of urgency and pressure to apply quickly, suggesting that there might be a loss if the student delays, which could be misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed pressure tactics suggesting urgency to apply to Galgotias, which could mislead the student regarding the necessity and timing of their application.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Galgotias ke liye na abhi sirf aap Galgotias visit karne mat aao.
incidents.translation: For Galgotias, I am saying that you should not just come to visit Galgotias.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is discouraging the student from exploring other colleges, which limits the student's ability to make an informed decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho tab tak fir na aapko aur difficulty ho jayegi, aapki agar Galgotias mein baat hui hai, same cheez, aisa hai ki main aapko chalo galat cheez suggest kar rahi hu.
incidents.translation: Look, by then it will become more difficult for you, if your discussion has happened with Galgotias, then it is like I am suggesting you the wrong thing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency that if the student does not register soon, they will face difficulties, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap 12th ke baad nahi nikloge, dekho generally student 17-18 saal ka ho jata hai jab tak wo 12th karta hai. Theek hai? Uske baad wo uske liye bahut mushkil time nahi hai bahar nikalna.
incidents.translation: If you do not go out after 12th, generally students become 17-18 years old by the time they finish 12th. Okay? After that, it is not a very difficult time for them to go out.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that not going to college outside will lead to difficulties in the future, which is an overpromise about the necessity of attending Galgotias specifically.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed multiple instances of misselling by discouraging the student from exploring other options and creating a false sense of urgency regarding registration, while also overpromising the benefits of attending Galgotias University.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To abhi ho sakta hai ke kyuki kya hota hai jab CUCET ka aap slot book karte ho to usme multiple date hoti hai, online exam hai to aap apne comfort ke according date book kar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: So it could happen that when you book a slot for CUCET, there are multiple dates available, it's an online exam so you can book a date according to your comfort.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the student can choose any date at their convenience, but later mentions limited slots and urgency, creating a false sense of scarcity.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But still aisa hua hai kai baar application kya hota hai close hone bhi wale hote hain na to website pe kabhi publish nahi hota ke hum application close kar rahe hain ya ho chuke hain ya seats full ho chuki hai.
incidents.translation: But it has happened many times that the application is about to close and it is never published on the website that we are closing applications or that seats are full.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates unnecessary pressure on the student to act quickly, suggesting that they might miss out on the opportunity without any evidence of actual scarcity.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics to create a sense of urgency regarding the application process, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.college_or_course: KCC Institute of Technology and Management
incidents.counsellor_quote: KCC ka bhi option leke chal sakte ho, KCC ka option aapko fee structure aapko kam hai, affordable hai. Easily aap isko afford kar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: You can also consider KCC as an option, the fee structure of KCC is low, it is affordable. You can easily afford it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the affordability of KCC without discussing the student's specific needs or preferences, which can be seen as a push to apply based on the low fee rather than the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Because iska application form abhi currently free hai.
incidents.translation: Because the application form is currently free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor highlights the free application as a primary reason to consider KIIT, which can mislead the student into thinking that the college is a good fit solely based on the lack of application fees.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on free applications and affordability without adequately addressing the student's specific needs indicates a push towards certain colleges that may not align with the student's interests.

incidents.counsellor_quote: isne jo apna highest salary package hota hai jo ki Indian basis pe agar dekhte hai to 54.75 LPA ka hai, agar international wala hota hai to isme 1.70 CR ka hai, like approx 2 CR ka isme salary package offer kar rahe hai ye log.
incidents.translation: The highest salary package, if we look at it based on Indian standards, is 54.75 LPA, and if it's international, then it's around 1.70 CR, like approximately 2 CR is the salary package they are offering.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is presenting inflated placement claims that are not substantiated, which can mislead the student about the actual job prospects and salary packages at the college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding placement packages, which could significantly influence the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Application form abhi basically iska abhi free hai. To easily apna apply kar sakti hain.
incidents.translation: The application form is currently free. So you can easily apply.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of finding a suitable college fit.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Sonipat mein aapko admission milna easy hai.
incidents.translation: It is easy to get admission in Sonipat.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim that admission is easy may mislead the student into thinking that acceptance is guaranteed without considering the actual admission criteria.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kal iske last date hai iska application form fill karne ka.
incidents.translation: Tomorrow is the last date to fill the application form.
incidents.why_misselling: The urgency created around the application deadline may pressure the student to apply without adequate research, especially if the urgency is not substantiated.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that emphasized free applications and easy admissions, creating a misleading sense of urgency and potentially swaying the student towards applying without thorough consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap Google pe check kar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: You can check on Google.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student can find confirmation of the MIT-WPU CET being valid for admission, despite the student stating that the college has stopped considering admissions through this exam. This misleads the student into believing there is still a viable path to admission that the college has actually closed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To matlab yahan Symbiosis se achha to koi aapko option hi dekhne ki zaroorat hi nahi hai.
incidents.translation: So if you get admission here, then you don't need to look for any better option than Symbiosis.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that Symbiosis is the only viable option, which pressures the student to consider it without exploring other possibilities.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the admission process for MIT-WPU and created a false sense of urgency regarding Symbiosis, which could lead the student to make uninformed decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: few colleges are there they are not charging anything.
incidents.translation: There are a few colleges that are not charging anything.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that some colleges have free applications as a primary reason to apply, which could mislead the student into thinking that cost is the only factor to consider.

incidents.counsellor_quote: if you will perform well in JEE Main examination or COMEDK they will provide you admission for that.
incidents.translation: If you perform well in the JEE Main examination or COMEDK, they will provide you admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that admission is guaranteed based on performance in entrance exams, which can be misleading as admission is not solely based on exam performance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about free applications and guaranteed admission based on exam performance, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Intellipaat is a very good college, very new and good option. They are having a very good placement data also.
incidents.translation: Intellipaat is a very good college, very new and good option. They have very good placement data as well.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim about the placement data being 'very good' lacks substantiation and could mislead the student into believing that placements are guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: these colleges are not charging anything as per now.
incidents.translation: These colleges are not charging anything as of now.
incidents.why_misselling: The phrase 'as per now' implies a temporary situation that could change, creating a false sense of urgency for the student to apply immediately without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about the college's placement data and created a sense of urgency regarding application fees that could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: main to ye suggest karunga ki bheed mein chalne se better hai ek safe zone mein bhi chal ke dekhe in Delhi NCR.
incidents.translation: I would suggest that instead of going with the crowd, it's better to explore a safe zone in Delhi NCR.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the student should act quickly to secure a place, which can create unnecessary pressure without a clear basis for the urgency.

incidents.counsellor_quote: ITS mein sabse pehle course close hote hain, main aapko pehle hi remind kar raha hoon is cheez ke liye.
incidents.translation: In ITS, the courses close first, I am reminding you of this.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement suggests a guaranteed admission scenario based on urgency, which can mislead the student into thinking they must act immediately to secure a spot.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of urgency around admissions and course availability could mislead the student into feeling pressured to make a quick decision without fully exploring their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi currently abhi off hai because abhi aapko off hue hain but seat limited hai to kab tak off rahenge iska koi fix abhi nahi hai.
incidents.translation: Currently, it is off because you just finished your exams, but the seats are limited, and there is no fixed time for how long it will remain off.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency by stating that the application form is currently free but that the seats are limited without providing evidence, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: iska bhi currently abhi application form abhi free hai.
incidents.translation: The application form is currently free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply without discussing the college's fit for the student, which can mislead the student into thinking the college is a good option solely based on the free application.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency and the appeal of a free application to push the student towards specific colleges without adequately addressing the student's needs or preferences.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko scholarship highest lene ke chances rehte hain.
incidents.translation: you have the chances of getting the highest scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying to multiple colleges will guarantee higher scholarship offers, which is misleading as it suggests a certainty that cannot be guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko sirf interviews dene hain, GD round dene hain aur offer letter uthana hai har university se.
incidents.translation: you just have to give interviews, do the GD round, and collect the offer letter from each university.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor simplifies the admission process and implies that it is easy to secure admission, which may create a false sense of urgency and ease.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarship opportunities and the admission process that could mislead the student into thinking they have guaranteed outcomes.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Achha main ek cheez aapko haan yahan pe mujhe ek information deni thi ki CGC ka agar aap form fill karte hain theek hai to Shiksha ki taraf se isme aapko 50% aapko matlab ki scholarship offer provide hota hai jo bhi amount hota hai aapka jo fees aayegi fees ka 50% aapko milta hai.
incidents.translation: I have one information to give you that if you fill the form for CGC, then Shiksha will provide you with a 50% scholarship on the total fees amount.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that a 50% scholarship is guaranteed if the student fills the form through Shiksha, which can be misleading as it conditions the scholarship on the form submission without clarifying that it may depend on other factors.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a misleading claim about a guaranteed scholarship, which could pressure the student into making a decision based on incomplete information.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To isliye main aapko keh rahi hoon ki aap at least ek aur college ka to application daal ke dekho, dono mein interview do, kya pata LPU se better aapko wahan pe kya kehte hain, scholarship wagera mil rahi ho.
incidents.translation: That's why I'm telling you that at least apply for one more college, give interviews for both, who knows you might get something better there than LPU, and there might be scholarships available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pressuring the student to apply to another college without acknowledging the student's clear preference for LPU, which could mislead the student into thinking they need to apply elsewhere to secure their admission.

incidents.counsellor_quote: uska abhi jo fees hai wo waived off chal raha hai, matlab ki abhi aapko application amount wagera nahi pay karna hai.
incidents.translation: The fees for that are currently waived off, which means you don't have to pay the application amount.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived fees as a primary reason to apply, which can be seen as a tactic to encourage application without focusing on the college's fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed pressure tactics to encourage the student to apply to additional colleges, which could mislead the student regarding their options and urgency.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To main suggest karungi ki agar as a backup ke liye bhi ek kuch aap colleges dekh rahi hain, to aap Amity University ke liye free of cost mein application form fill kar sakti hain, theek hai?
incidents.translation: So I would suggest that if you are looking for some backup options, you can fill the application form for Amity University free of cost, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free of cost' application as a primary reason to apply, rather than focusing on the suitability of the college for the student's needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar PI aapne achhe se handle kiya to then us basis pe colleges jo hain aapko admission provide karte hain, theek hai?
incidents.translation: If you handle the PI well, then based on that, the colleges will provide you admission, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that handling the personal interview well guarantees admission, which is misleading as admission is not solely based on the interview performance.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Yahan pe aa raha hai ki Mithibai College offers limited seats for its offered courses.
incidents.translation: It is mentioned here that Mithibai College offers limited seats for its offered courses.
incidents.why_misselling: The mention of limited seats creates a sense of urgency without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included several instances of misselling, particularly emphasizing free applications and creating urgency around limited seats, which could mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: abhi na Amity University ke kuch kuch campus mein na fees waive hai matlab application charge nahi lagega aapka, correct?
incidents.translation: Currently, at some campuses of Amity University, the fees are waived, meaning there will be no application charge for you, correct?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fees as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits, which can mislead the student into thinking applying is a no-cost decision without considering other factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: phase one mein apply karne ke fayde zyada hote hain as compared to phase two, ek to scholarship zyada milti hai, campus mein seat hoti hai to aapka desire jo specialization hai jo aap choose karna chah rahe ho usme aapko admission milne ke zyada chance rahenge.
incidents.translation: The benefits of applying in phase one are more compared to phase two; one is that you get more scholarships, and there are seats available in the campus, so you will have a higher chance of getting admission in your desired specialization.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that applying in phase one is significantly better than phase two without providing concrete evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on waived fees and the urgency to apply in phase one could mislead the student into thinking these are the most critical factors in their decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Even there are many colleges jinka application bhi abhi like non-chargeable hai, that means application charges free of cost hai, to aap woh colleges ke liye bhi check kar sakte ho because unki again seats to limited hi hoti hain Ankit.
incidents.translation: Even there are many colleges whose applications are currently non-chargeable, that means application charges are free of cost, so you can check those colleges because their seats are limited.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency by suggesting that the free application is tied to limited seats, which may not be true, creating pressure to apply quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about limited seats for free applications creates a sense of urgency that may not be justified, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Chalo theek hai to main ye close yahan se nahi kar rahi hoon kyunki save I guess save to ho gaya hoga sara poora 100% steps complete ho gaye last baki hai bas aapka.
incidents.translation: Okay, so I am not closing this here because I guess it has been saved, all the steps are 100% complete, the last part is just yours.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the process is nearly complete and creates a sense of urgency to finalize the application, which may not be true, as the student still needs to make a payment.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Galgotia aap mujhe confirmation de dijiyega payment ke baad, Shiksha ke end se cashback ka offer rahega ispe theek hai.
incidents.translation: After you confirm the payment for Galgotia, there will be a cashback offer from Shiksha.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that confirming payment will lead to a cashback offer, which may mislead the student into thinking that payment guarantees a benefit that is not clearly defined.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of the application process and the mention of cashback offers create a misleading sense of urgency and assurance about benefits, which could sway the student's decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh main wo form fill karungi cashback ke liye.
incidents.translation: So I will fill the form for cashback for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is promoting a cashback offer as an incentive to complete the payment, which could mislead the student into thinking this is a guaranteed benefit rather than a possibility.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's mention of cashback as a benefit during the payment process could be seen as a form of misselling, as it may create an impression of guaranteed financial return.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to wo to matlab ye hai ki agar aapko wahan se mil gaya to wo to matlab aapke paas golden opportunity rehti hai pata hai fees kitni hai 7 se 8 lakh rupaye hi fees lagti hai usme.
incidents.translation: So, this means that if you get in there, it will be a golden opportunity for you. Do you know how much the fees are? It's only 7 to 8 lakh rupees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that admission to VIT Vellore is a guaranteed 'golden opportunity', which can mislead the student into thinking that admission is highly likely without acknowledging the competitive nature of the entrance exam.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ab form ab close ho jayenge April mein aapka uska paper hona hai.
incidents.translation: The forms will close in April, and your exam will be then.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the forms will close soon, which may not be accurate or necessary, pressuring the student to act quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about VIT Vellore create misleading expectations about admission and urgency, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki CSE ka aaj ka aapko bhi pata hai na, CSE aaj ke date mein demandable hai sabse zyada.
incidents.translation: Because you also know that CSE is the most in-demand course today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that CSE is highly in-demand, suggesting that the student must act quickly to secure a seat, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To abhi registration karke chalo nahi to baad mein aisa na ho ki aap pay bhi kar diya, registration paid hota hai, 1000 rupees ka registration aata hai, baad mein aap registration pay bhi kar diya uske baad aapka profile call nahi aa raha hai college se, waiting mein chala jayega to zyada painful hota hai.
incidents.translation: So register now, otherwise later you might pay and then your profile won't get a call from the college, and it will go into waiting, which will be more painful.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that failing to register immediately could lead to a negative outcome, creating undue pressure on the student to act quickly without providing a balanced view of the situation.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics and created a sense of urgency to push the student towards immediate registration, which can be considered manipulative and misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But agar free of cost application hai to aap fill matlab aap itna kyu soch rahe hai cheezon ke liye.
incidents.translation: But if the application is free of cost, then why are you thinking so much about it?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is using the fact that the application is free as a primary reason to encourage the student to apply, rather than discussing the suitability of the college or course.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application without addressing the student's concerns about scholarships and suitability indicates a nudge towards applying without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho thoda sa pain hoga, aapko thoda sa aap door jaoge, lekin aapke liye better rahega kyunki aur saare VITs mein wo wala VIT kafi achha hai.
incidents.translation: Look, it will be a bit of a pain, you will have to go a bit far, but it will be better for you because among all the VITs, that VIT is quite good.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency and necessity to choose VIT Vellore over other options without providing a balanced view of alternatives, which can mislead the student into thinking it's the only viable option.

incidents.counsellor_quote: VIT Vellore mein seats bhi limited hoti hain aur seats limited hoti hain aur haan specialization ke according aapko seats bhi nahi milengi.
incidents.translation: There are limited seats in VIT Vellore, and according to specialization, you may not get seats.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that seats are limited without providing evidence, which can pressure the student to apply quickly without considering other options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed pressure tactics by emphasizing limited seats and the necessity of applying to VIT Vellore, which could mislead the student regarding their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko Shiksha ki taraf se 200 rupees ka cashback amount applicable hai aapke liye aaj ke liye.
incidents.translation: You will be eligible for a cashback amount of 200 rupees from Shiksha for today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is promoting a cashback offer as a primary incentive to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking this is a significant benefit rather than focusing on the college's actual merits.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To ab aapke paas option kya bacha ki aap in private universities mein most of the universities ko target karo.
incidents.translation: So now you have the option to target most of the private universities.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying to these universities is a guaranteed way to secure admission, which can mislead the student about the competitive nature of admissions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's promotion of cashback and the implication of guaranteed admission create a misleading narrative for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To Amity University Mumbai ke liye aap free of cost mein application bhar sakte hain. Unka kuch exam bhi nahi hoga. Bas ek aapka interview hoga aur interview ke basis pe fir wahan pe admission milta hai.
incidents.translation: For Amity University Mumbai, you can fill the application free of cost. There is no exam. Only an interview will be conducted, and based on that, you will get admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free and downplays the importance of the interview, which could mislead the student into thinking admission is guaranteed without a proper assessment.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap mujhe ye bataiye ki aapka koi matlab abhi tak kisi college ke liye form fill kiya hai aapne, kisi bhi college ke liye?
incidents.translation: Please tell me if you have filled any application form for any college yet?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by asking if the student has already filled forms, suggesting that time is running out, which may not be the case.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used misleading claims about free applications and created a sense of urgency that could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: free of cost mein unka matlab Mumbai ke campus ka aap free of cost mein application form hai.
incidents.translation: The application form for the Mumbai campus is free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the 'free of cost' application as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that this is the most important factor rather than the college's fit for their needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko achha khasa time milega admission lene ke liye.
incidents.translation: You will have a good amount of time to take admission.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement implies a guarantee of admission and suggests that the student will have ample time to decide, which may not be the case.

incidents.counsellor_quote: dekhiye kal Sunday hai.
incidents.translation: See, tomorrow is Sunday.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor uses the upcoming Sunday as a reason to pressure the student into providing details quickly, implying urgency without a valid reason.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application and the implications of guaranteed admission create a misleading narrative that could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Phase one to close hone wala hai. Uska shayad abhi last date aapka paanch che din baad hi hai.
incidents.translation: Phase one is about to close. Its last date is probably in five to six days.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the deadline is imminent without providing concrete evidence, which may pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap profiling mein eligible ho jate ho to offer letter aa jayega aapka.
incidents.translation: If you are eligible in profiling, your offer letter will come.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that simply being eligible will guarantee an offer letter, which misrepresents the admission process and creates a false sense of security.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited clear instances of misselling by creating false urgency and overpromising admission outcomes, which could mislead the student into making rushed decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Last date iska first come first serve pe aaj hai. First come first serve slot ki aaj hai. Jisme aapko admission mil hi jaayega.
incidents.translation: The last date is today for the first come first serve. If you apply today, you will definitely get admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the last date is today and implies that applying today guarantees admission, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Matlab aaj agar bhar diya toh admission confirm hai lagbhag? Haan ji haan ji lagbhag lagbhag interview ke baad.
incidents.translation: If you apply today, admission is almost confirmed after the interview.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees that admission will be almost confirmed if the student applies today, which is a blatant false claim.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about application deadlines and guaranteed admission, which constitutes clear cases of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aap mujhe bata do fir aapne abhi bola ki B.Arch ke liye aap dekh, B.Arch ke liye bhi deke aaye ho toh aapki preference kya hai? Aap technical like Computer Science and all karna chahte ho ya B.Arch se related dekhna chahte ho?
incidents.translation: You tell me then, you just said that you are looking for B.Arch, so what is your preference? Do you want to pursue something technical like Computer Science or do you want to look for something related to B.Arch?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pushing the student towards a specific course (B.Arch) without acknowledging the student's clear preference for Computer Science, which could lead to a mismatch in the student's educational goals.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh KIIT mein aap directly abhi apply kar sakte ho. Second phase I think aaj close ho jayega. Sorry, first phase aaj close ho jayega.
incidents.translation: So you can apply directly to KIIT now. I think the second phase will close today. Sorry, the first phase will close today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the application phase is closing today, which may not be true or verified, pressuring the student to act quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited signs of misselling by pushing for specific courses and creating a false sense of urgency regarding application deadlines.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ritika ye aapka WhatsApp number hai kya? Haan main ek baar dekh lungi. Haan wo maine aapko share kar diya aap ek baar check karo and iske saath saath main aapko application form share kar du? Haan main ek baar dekh lungi. Haan ye aapka GNIOT ka maine aapko share kar diya hai ek baar aap check kariye and saath saath iska application form bhi main aapko share kar deti hu. Ye jo detail hai isme se sabse important abhi aapke liye hai wo Amity because iska last date aapka aaj ka hai.
incidents.translation: Ritika, is this your WhatsApp number? Yes, I will check once. Yes, I have shared it with you, please check and I will also share the application form with you. Yes, I will check once. Yes, I have shared the GNIOT details with you, please check and I will also share the application form with you. This detail is the most important for you right now because the last date for Amity is today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the last date for Amity University is today, which may pressure the student to make a quick decision without adequate research.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the urgency of the application deadline for Amity University could mislead the student into feeling rushed to apply without fully considering their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki dekho, I think agar aapki pehle baat hui hai to exam ke base pe aap eligible ho jaate ho scholarship ke liye bhi.
incidents.translation: Because I think if you have talked earlier, then based on the exam, you become eligible for scholarships as well.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that taking the entrance exam will guarantee eligibility for scholarships, which may not be true and can mislead the student into thinking they will receive financial aid based solely on their exam performance.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To better hai ke agar aapke paas abhi option hai, abhi aap woh option avail kar lo.
incidents.translation: It's better that if you have the option now, you should avail that option now.
