incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the opportunity to avail options may not last, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements regarding scholarship eligibility and the urgency to act create a misleading impression that could pressure the student into making decisions without fully understanding their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To sabse pehle jo bhi South ke top colleges hain, jo aapke mind mein hain ya jo aapka bachcha bhi jisme interested hai, aap minimum, kam se kam at least char colleges ke ya paanch colleges ke form bhariye.
incidents.translation: So first of all, for all the top colleges in the South that are in your mind or that your child is interested in, you should fill out the forms for at least four or five colleges.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that filling out multiple college applications will guarantee admission, which is misleading as admission is based on merit and not merely on the number of applications submitted.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To first phase mein jo selection hoga ya shortlisting hogi na application ki, to usi criteria ke according judge kiya jayega. To possibility first phase mein zyada high hoti hai as compared to second phase.
incidents.translation: So the selection or shortlisting in the first phase of applications will be judged according to that criteria. So the possibility is much higher in the first phase compared to the second phase.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a false sense of urgency by implying that applying in the first phase is significantly more advantageous, which may not be substantiated by actual admission processes.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements about the necessity of applying to multiple colleges for guaranteed admission and the urgency of the first application phase are misleading and could pressure the student into hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh main agar aapko suggest karoon toh aap result ka wait kar lo theek hai lekin aap kisi university mein registration kar sakte ho jaise aapne isme Manipal mein kiya hai, aap CGC mein bhi kar lo.
incidents.translation: So if I suggest you, wait for the result, but you can register in any university like you did in Manipal, you can also do it in CGC.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency to register at CGC University before the results are out, creating a sense of pressure without clear justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh 75% above marks, sorry attendance hote hain aapke toh aap isme like aapka 10% additional discount mil jayega, baaki aap jo entrance doge toh usme mil jaata hai.
incidents.translation: So if you have 75% above marks, sorry attendance, then you will get an additional 10% discount, and you will also get it if you give the entrance.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the scholarship as a guaranteed benefit based on attendance, which may not be achievable for the student, thus misleading them.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a sense of urgency and misrepresent the conditions for scholarships, which could mislead the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aisa kuch nahi hota. Aisa kuch nahi hota. Aisa kuch problem nahi.
incidents.translation: Nothing like that happens. There is no such problem.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor downplays the urgency created by the university's request for immediate payment, suggesting that the student can take their time, which contradicts the university's claim and creates confusion about the actual urgency.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapka na ek hisaab se seat confirm ho jaata hai.
incidents.translation: Your seat gets confirmed in a way.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that paying the amount will guarantee a seat, which can mislead the student into thinking that payment is the only requirement for admission confirmation.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and imply guaranteed admission upon payment, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Seats abhi hain, but fast fill ho rahi hain. Isliye main suggest karunga ki aap application process jald se jald complete kar lein.
incidents.translation: There are seats available, but they are filling fast. That's why I would suggest that you complete the application process as soon as possible.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency about seat availability without providing evidence or specific details, which can pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding seat availability, which could mislead the student into thinking they need to act quickly without clear justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: So abhi inke jo application form jo hain wo 31st of March yaani ki kal tak free hai, theek hai? So I will be suggesting ki aaj ya kal mein agar aap apna application bhar lete ho.
incidents.translation: So currently their application forms are free until the 31st of March, okay? So I will be suggesting that you fill your application today or tomorrow.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the application is only free for a limited time, which may pressure the student to apply without considering other options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap jis bhi cheez mein comfortable ho, aap usme de sakte ho, online mode aapko ghar pe baith ke dena hoga but uske liye aapko laptop wagera chahiye hoga.
incidents.translation: So you can give the exam in whichever mode you are comfortable with, online mode you will have to give from home but for that you will need a laptop.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student can easily take the exam from home, which may not accurately reflect the actual requirements or conditions of the exam.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed instances of creating urgency around application deadlines and made claims about the ease of exam conditions that could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aaj only for today the forms are free so that's why.
incidents.translation: Today only for today the forms are free so that's why.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free only for today, which could pressure the student into applying without considering other options or the actual value of the college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's insistence on the urgency of applying today due to the free application could lead to hasty decisions by the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aap do ya teen din bhi delay karoge na to main literally keh raha hu ki might be aapko CSE mein seats na mile.
incidents.translation: If you delay by even two or three days, I am literally saying that you might not get seats in CSE.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that seats may not be available soon, which could be misleading if there is no actual evidence of scarcity.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho application form apply karne se basically hota kya hai ki admission ki possibilities na kaafi had tak badh jaati hain.
incidents.translation: See, what happens when you apply for the application form is that the possibilities of admission increase significantly.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that simply applying increases the chances of admission significantly, which may not be a guaranteed outcome.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise the likelihood of admission, which can pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aur abhi even agar aap application bhi apply karte ho na to iska application fee jo hai na wo bhi waive off hai, free of cost hai abhi application fee SOA ka.
incidents.translation: And even if you apply for the application now, the application fee is waived off, it's free of cost for the SOA application.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application fee as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits, which can mislead the student into thinking that the college is a better option solely due to the waived fee.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap apply kar sakte ho agar aapko option mein rakhna hai to SOA rakh sakte ho.
incidents.translation: So you can apply if you want to keep SOA as an option.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests applying to SOA without adequately addressing the student's qualifications or the competitive nature of admissions, which can imply an easier admission process than may actually exist.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the free application fee and the suggestion to apply without discussing the student's qualifications raises concerns about potential misrepresentation of the admission process and the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aaj ke date mein matlab aaj ke liye uski application ki koi charges nahi lag rahe hain. Woh early bird kyunki kal se JEE Mains ke exam shuru ho rahe hain.
incidents.translation: As of today, there are no charges for the application. This is an early bird offer because the JEE Mains exams are starting tomorrow.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by linking the application fee waiver to the upcoming JEE Mains exams, which may not be a legitimate reason for the fee waiver and could pressure the student to apply quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Somaiya Vidyavihar ki jo NIRF ranking hai 201 and 300 hai.
incidents.translation: The NIRF ranking of Somaiya Vidyavihar is between 201 and 300.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the NIRF ranking in a way that may mislead the student into thinking it is a strong indicator of quality, without clarifying that being in the 201-300 range is not particularly prestigious.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used urgency related to application fees and presented rankings in a potentially misleading way, which could influence the student's decision-making.

incidents.counsellor_quote: sirf paanch hi coupon codes available hai, theek hai.
incidents.translation: only five coupon codes are available, okay.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by claiming that only five coupon codes are available, which may not be true and pressures the student to apply quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the limited availability of coupon codes is misleading and creates unnecessary urgency for the student to apply.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar bacche ne perform achha kiya na entrance exam mein to maybe unki puri fees waived off ho jayegi.
incidents.translation: If the child performs well in the entrance exam, then maybe their entire fees will be waived off.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the entire fee could be waived off based on performance in the entrance exam, which can mislead the student into thinking that this is a guaranteed outcome without clarifying the conditions or likelihood.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a potentially misleading claim regarding the scholarship availability at Pillai Business School, which could create unrealistic expectations for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki unka entrance exam Pulse ka hai jiske basis pe 100% ke chances rehte hain scholarship ke.
incidents.translation: because their entrance exam Pulse has a 100% chance of scholarship based on it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a guaranteed scholarship based on the entrance exam, which is misleading as it suggests certainty without acknowledging that scholarships are typically contingent on various factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To mere hisaab se agar aap chahte hain kyunki dekhiye aapko sabse pehle to Pillai Business School ke application pe 200 ka cashback hai.
incidents.translation: So in my opinion, if you want, because look, first of all, there is a cashback of 200 on the application for Pillai Business School.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the cashback as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the financial incentive is more important than the suitability of the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: kyunki jo unka Pulse ka entrance exam hai jiske basis pe wo 100% ki scholarship de rahe hain, uski date bahut near by hai.
incidents.translation: because the date for their Pulse entrance exam, on the basis of which they are offering 100% scholarship, is very near.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency regarding the entrance exam date without providing clear evidence that the date is imminent or that the scholarship is guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several misleading claims regarding scholarships and created a sense of urgency that could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Hum fill karne ka main reason sirf ek hai ki hum 100% scholarship wale round me kar de.
incidents.translation: The main reason for filling out the application is that we can get you into the 100% scholarship round.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that applying now guarantees entry into a scholarship round, which may not be true and could mislead the student into thinking they will definitely receive a scholarship.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko 48 hours me call aayegi, theek hai?
incidents.translation: You will receive a call within 48 hours, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that a call will definitely come within 48 hours, which may not be guaranteed and could pressure the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create misleading urgency and imply guaranteed scholarship opportunities, which could mislead the student regarding their application and financial aid prospects.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho JP me na aapko chances rehte hain scholarship ke, kyunki dekho kisi bhi college ki jo ye fee structure hai na this is not the final fee structure.
incidents.translation: Look, you have chances of getting a scholarship at JP, because the fee structure of any college is not the final fee structure.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the fee structure is negotiable based on scholarship availability, which may not be guaranteed and can mislead the student into thinking they will receive a scholarship without clear criteria.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekh apply karne ke baad jab tak aapka JEE Mains ka score aap JEE Mains agar aap dena chahte hain to.
incidents.translation: Look, after applying, until your JEE Mains score, if you want to give JEE Mains.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying to these colleges is straightforward and implies that admission is likely without clarifying the actual admission criteria or competition involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarship availability and admission processes that could mislead the student regarding their chances and the nature of the fees.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Application ki cost abhi waived off hai. Aapko application karne ke liye koi paise nahi dene padenge.
incidents.translation: The application cost is currently waived off. You don't have to pay anything to apply.
incidents.why_misselling: While it's normal to mention waived application fees, the counsellor emphasizes this as a primary reason to apply without discussing the college's fit or quality, which can mislead the student into thinking the college is a better option solely based on the free application.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aapko provisional letter dekh ke lagta hai ki haan yeh mere budget me fall karega to aap admission lena.
incidents.translation: If you feel that the provisional letter indicates it falls within your budget, then you can take admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that receiving a provisional letter guarantees admission if the budget is met, which can mislead the student into thinking admission is assured based on this condition.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 15 April ke aas paas ka entrance hoga, entrance de dena.
incidents.translation: The entrance will be around 15 April, so give the entrance exam.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency around the entrance exam date without providing clear evidence or context, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included several instances of emphasizing free applications and creating urgency, which could mislead the student regarding the quality and fit of the colleges discussed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: They will definitely call you and let you know ki kya aage ke liye process kya rahega.
incidents.translation: They will definitely call you and let you know what the next process will be.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's use of 'definitely' implies a guarantee of follow-up communication regarding the admission process, which may not be accurate.

incidents.counsellor_quote: KIIT ka jo entrance exam hai woh kaafi saare colleges tak accept karte hain uska score apne yahan par admission ke liye.
incidents.translation: The entrance exam of KIIT is accepted by many colleges for admission here.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement could mislead the student into thinking that applying to KIIT guarantees admission to other colleges based on its entrance exam, which is not necessarily true.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made statements that could mislead the student regarding the certainty of follow-up and the implications of the KIIT entrance exam, which could create undue pressure to apply.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To matlab jo sabse top pe placements agar aa rahi hai, woh Amity ki hi hai.
incidents.translation: So, the best placements are coming from Amity.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is discouraging the student from considering other colleges by implying that only Amity offers the best placements, which is misleading as it does not acknowledge the potential of other institutions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Lekin Amity mein aapko jo hai paise nahi lagte hain aur aapka form fill up ho jaata hai.
incidents.translation: But at Amity, you don't have to pay anything, and your form gets filled.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement suggests that the application process is completely free at Amity, which may not be true and could mislead the student about the costs involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the advantages of Amity University over other colleges and suggested that the application process was free, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Uski aaj ki last date hai aur shayad ek coupon code hoga mere paas to shayad without paying anything wo bhi submit ho sakta hai.
incidents.translation: Today is the last date for that, and I might have a coupon code so it might be submitted without paying anything.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that today is the last date for the application, which could pressure the student to act quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about the last date for SRM University creates unnecessary pressure on the student to apply immediately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: is college me Computer Science ke liye total 120 seats hain aur abhi lagbhag 40 seats hi bachi hain.
incidents.translation: In this college, there are a total of 120 seats for Computer Science and currently, only about 40 seats are left.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's statement about the number of seats left appears to create a sense of urgency without clear evidence, which can pressure the student to apply quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's mention of limited seats seems designed to create urgency, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply immediately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh ek baar dekho aap registration form open karo apna.
incidents.translation: So just check your registration form.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting the student should open the registration form immediately, creating a sense of pressure to act quickly without providing substantial reasons for this urgency.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh ye jo exam hai ye aapka online hi exam hota hai. Same jaise CU mein CU CET ka hota hai, CGC mein CGC UET ka exam hota hai.
incidents.translation: So this exam is an online exam. Just like CU has CU CET, CGC has CGC UET.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the CGC exam as equivalent to the CU exam without clarifying the differences or the actual admission criteria, which could mislead the student into thinking the admission process is similarly straightforward.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor displayed some pressure tactics and made claims that could mislead the student regarding the urgency and equivalence of the admission processes.

incidents.counsellor_quote: iska na up to 100% tak scholarship hai.
incidents.translation: It has up to 100% scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the scholarship as a significant incentive without clarifying the conditions or likelihood of obtaining it, which can mislead the student into thinking they are guaranteed financial support.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar main iska ranking wise baat karu to India ke top 10 colleges ke andar aa jaata hai yeh.
incidents.translation: If I talk about its ranking, it comes within the top 10 colleges in India.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims that the college is among the top 10 in India without providing evidence or context, which can mislead the student regarding the college's actual standing.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarships and rankings that could mislead the student regarding the college's offerings and reputation.

incidents.counsellor_quote: is ki na aaj expire ho jayega coupon code. Sirf aaj aaj ke liye update hua hai.
incidents.translation: the coupon code will expire today. It has been updated only for today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by claiming the coupon code is only valid for today without providing evidence, which pressures the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's claim about the coupon code's expiration creates undue pressure on the student to apply immediately, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki abhi jo hai bahut saare jo universities hai, uske na application close ho rahe hain.
incidents.translation: Because many universities are closing their applications right now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency regarding application deadlines without providing specific evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko jo hai scholarship milta hai.
incidents.translation: You will receive a scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student will definitely receive a scholarship based on their application, which is misleading as scholarships are typically conditional and not guaranteed.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited signs of misselling by creating a sense of urgency and overpromising outcomes related to scholarships, which could mislead the student into making rushed decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap late karoge July, May tak tab tak to mostly colleges ke jitne bhi acche colleges hai. Acche ki baat kar raha hu main. Acche kuch hi hote hai, baaki sab to hote hai sirf. Unka hona na hona ek hi barabar hota hai.
incidents.translation: If you delay until July or May, then mostly the good colleges will be gone. I am talking about good colleges. There are only a few good ones, the rest are just there. Their existence or non-existence is the same.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that good colleges will no longer be available if the student waits until the actual admission period, which is misleading.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko call aayega. You will also get a chance ki aap Amity written test de sakte ho aur kyunki aapke academics acche hai to aapko scholarship ke liye bhi scenario mil sakta hai yahan pe.
incidents.translation: You will get a call. You will also get a chance to take the Amity written test, and because your academics are good, you might also get a scenario for a scholarship here.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is implying that the student is guaranteed a call and a chance for a scholarship based solely on their academics, which is misleading and creates false expectations.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used manipulative language to create urgency and overpromised admission opportunities, which are clear cases of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To ab kya hota hai ki jo companies aati hai wo bahar ki bhi aati hai hiring karne ke liye B.Tech mein hi, CSE background mein, theek hai.
incidents.translation: So what happens is that the companies that come also come from abroad for hiring in B.Tech, especially in the CSE background.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that international companies specifically seek graduates from Amity University, which may not be substantiated and creates an inflated perception of placement opportunities.

incidents.counsellor_quote: average packages 8 se 9 lakh ka hai, highest jo gaya hai wo 1.5 crore ka gaya hai, lekin wo international placement tha.
incidents.translation: The average packages are between 8 to 9 lakhs, and the highest went up to 1.5 crores, but that was an international placement.
incidents.why_misselling: The mention of the highest package being 1.5 crores without context can mislead students into thinking such placements are common, which may not reflect the typical outcomes for most students.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To jaise agar college mein aap application form fill kar loge to isse fayda ye rehta hai ki agar maan lo aapko 95 ya 96 percentile aa raha hai, theek hai.
incidents.translation: So if you fill the application form for the college, the benefit is that if you get a 95 or 96 percentile, then...
incidents.why_misselling: This statement suggests that simply filling out the application guarantees consideration for admission, which can mislead the student about the admission process and criteria.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several misleading claims regarding placement opportunities and the admission process, which could significantly sway the student's decision-making.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to maine socha ek baar aapko update de doon kyunki isliye free hua hai aaj 31st March hai na, last date hai.
incidents.translation: So I thought I should give you an update because today is the last date for applying as the form has become free.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by stating that today is the last date for applying, which could pressure the student to apply without considering other options, especially since the claim of the form being free is used as a primary reason to apply.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency around the application deadline, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapne jaise already Pillai ka fill kar liya hai na, toh agar aap Amity ka fill karenge toh ab aapko uske liye koi payment nahi karni padegi.
incidents.translation: Since you have already filled the Pillai application, if you fill the Amity application, you won't have to make any payment for it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free as the primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the college is a good fit without discussing other important factors.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application may mislead the student about the value and fit of Amity University without providing a comprehensive view of the college's offerings.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi aaj inka nahi dikha raha hai, it means ki seats almost full ho chuki hai to isiliye shayad options nahi aa rahe hain.
incidents.translation: Right now, it is not showing availability, which means that seats are almost full, that's why options are not appearing.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that seats are almost full without providing evidence, creating a sense of urgency for the student to apply immediately.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To inke mein bhi almost seats full hone wali hai, to inka bhi karna hoga to aaj mein hi aapko karna hoga.
incidents.translation: For these as well, seats are almost full, so you will have to do it today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that seats are filling up quickly, pressuring the student to act immediately without clear evidence.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used misleading urgency regarding seat availability to pressure the student into applying quickly, which constitutes misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: seats bahut kam bachi thi usmein.
incidents.translation: There were very few seats left in that.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency regarding seat availability without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap apply karo aur baad mein other options bhi to main aapko dilwa hi rahi hoon.
incidents.translation: You apply and later I will also get you other options.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that applying will guarantee access to other options, which can mislead the student into thinking that admission is assured.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a sense of urgency and imply guarantees about admission that are misleading.

incidents.college_or_course: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology (KIIT) and Siksha 'O' Anusandhan (SOA)

incidents.counsellor_quote: Iska kar lo kyunki time's up ho raha hai, two minutes remaining bacha hai.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 15th ka iska last hai. Apply 15 se pehle pehle kar do aap kyunki phase one ka aaya hai jisme up to 100% tak ki scholarship hai.
incidents.translation: The last date for this is the 15th. You should apply before the 15th because phase one has come where up to 100% scholarship is available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating a deadline that may not be genuine, pressuring the student to apply quickly without sufficient information.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh yeh bahut achha university hai. Apply karo aur yahan pe na entrance test hota hai jisme up to 100% tak ki scholarship bhi milti hai.
incidents.translation: So this is a very good university. Apply and here there is an entrance test where you can get up to 100% scholarship.

incidents.college_or_course: SRM University, UEM Kolkata, NSHM Bangalore, SOA
incidents.counsellor_quote: But mere paas ab sabke coupon code hain available. Theek hai to sab mein koi bhi fees nahi lagega, free of cost hi sir sabka apply ho jayega.
incidents.translation: But I have all the coupon codes available now. So there will be no fees in any of them, you can apply free of cost.
