incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi unke na unko batao hi mat abhi na.
incidents.translation: No, don't tell them about it right now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor advises the student not to inform others about the applications, which discourages the student from comparing options and making an informed decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach includes misleading claims about free applications and discouraging the student from discussing options with others, indicating a push towards applying without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: VIT ka 31st is the last date. But VIT aaj close ho jayega.
incidents.translation: The last date for VIT is 31st. But VIT will close today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by stating that the application for VIT will close today, which is misleading as the actual deadline is later.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kalinga ka mere paas abhi coupon code hai. To coupon code main aapko share kar dungi. To waha se aapka exam mein help ho jayega, sorry, application form mein discount mil jayega to free of cost mein hi apply ho jayega aapka.
incidents.translation: I have a coupon code for Kalinga. I will share the coupon code with you. This will help you in the exam, sorry, you will get a discount on the application form, so you can apply free of cost.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the coupon code as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of the college's fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used misleading urgency regarding application deadlines and emphasized a free application offer to push the student towards applying.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To fir main yahi bolunga fir full fledge aap B.Tech hi course karwao.
incidents.translation: So I will say that you should go for the B.Tech course fully.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is strongly suggesting that the student should pursue B.Tech without acknowledging the student's specific needs or interests, which could be seen as pushing a course without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki ab na season aa chuka hai, sabke na 12th khatam ho chuke hai aur na pehle jo colleges me form fill up hote the wo 10-20 bacche aate the form fill karke jaate the, ab jo hai wahi flow badh gaya hai.
incidents.translation: Because now the season has started, all the 12th students have completed their exams, and previously only 10-20 students used to come to fill forms, but now the flow has increased.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that many students are applying, which may not be substantiated, pressuring the user to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited signs of pushing the B.Tech course at Amity University while creating a sense of urgency, which could mislead the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity University already apna jo application hai wo 31st March ko close kar rahi thi phase one ka. But 15 days ka unhone extension diya hai, kyunki 31st ki raat ko kuch zyada students apply kar rahe the jiski wajah se unhone extension deke 15 days ka badha ke 15th of April tak ke liye kar diya.
incidents.translation: Amity University was closing its application for phase one on March 31. But they have given a 15-day extension because many students were applying on the night of the 31st, which is why they extended it to April 15.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the extension as a response to high demand, which may not be substantiated, creating a sense of urgency that could pressure the student into applying quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Agar aap 90 se kam aayega to apply karne ka koi benefit nahi hai kyunki phir aapko jo college milenge wo outskirts pe milenge, Bahadurgarh hai, Faridabad hai, ya phir aapka Sonipat hai, phir aisi locations pe colleges milenge IPU ke.
incidents.translation: If you score below 90, there is no benefit in applying because then the colleges you will get will be on the outskirts, like Bahadurgarh, Faridabad, or Sonipat.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that only high scores guarantee admission to desirable colleges, which may not be accurate and could discourage the student from applying to other options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and make overpromising claims about admission prospects based on scores, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 94.2 to ma'am definitely bacche ka admission ho jayega yahan par.
incidents.translation: 94.2, ma'am, your child will definitely get admission here.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission based on a percentile that is below the stated cutoff, which is misleading and creates false expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 3 baje se pehle pehle apply karna. Kyunki 4 baje campus close ho jata hai.
incidents.translation: Apply before 3 PM. Because the campus closes at 4 PM.
incidents.why_misselling: The urgency created by stating that the campus closes at a specific time without evidence of a real deadline is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding admission guarantees and created a false sense of urgency, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: bacche ka score bahut accha hai, score hai matlab accha score hai to Thapar se call aa jayega bacche ko definitely.
incidents.translation: The child's score is very good, it means a good score, so the child will definitely get a call from Thapar.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees a call from Thapar based solely on the student's score, which is misleading as admission decisions can depend on various factors beyond just the score.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a definitive promise regarding admission based on the student's score, which constitutes misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kyunki almost har bacche ne apply kar chuka hai. Kyunki close ho jayenge na aaj hi application forms.
incidents.translation: Because almost every student has applied. Because the application forms will close today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the application forms will close today without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's use of urgency regarding the application deadline is misleading and could pressure the student into applying without proper consideration.

incidents.counsellor_quote: VIT to 31st of March ko close kar rahe hain.
incidents.translation: VIT is closing on 31st of March.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies an urgent deadline for VIT without providing evidence or context, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding the application deadline for VIT, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply without sufficient information.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Chandigarh University ka placement record kaafi achha hai, main aapko bata deta hoon. Achha. Jo highest, highest package gaya tha wo 1.7 CR ka gaya tha aapka.
incidents.translation: Chandigarh University's placement record is quite good, I can tell you. The highest package that went was around 1.7 CR.
incidents.why_misselling: The claim of a 1.7 CR package is misleading as it is not representative of the average experience of students and may create unrealistic expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Iska jo highest package gaya tha wo 1 CR ka gaya tha aapka.
incidents.translation: The highest package that went was around 1 CR.
incidents.why_misselling: Similar to the previous incident, this claim may mislead the student into thinking that such high packages are common, which could pressure them into choosing this college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several claims about placement packages that could mislead the student regarding the actual placement scenario, creating inflated expectations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko kaafi high chances hote hai up to 100% tak scholarship ke.
incidents.translation: you have quite high chances of up to 100% scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student has a high chance of receiving a 100% scholarship, which may not be guaranteed and depends on merit, thus creating a misleading impression of certainty.

incidents.counsellor_quote: placement rate ki agar main baat karu to around 97 98% iska placement rate hai.
incidents.translation: if I talk about the placement rate, it is around 97-98%.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents placement rates that may not be verified or accurate, which can mislead the student about the college's effectiveness in securing jobs for graduates.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarship chances and placement rates that could mislead the student regarding the actual opportunities and outcomes at VIT.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Placement, placement records ki main agar baat karun Manipal ki to woh around 99.99 percentage hai.
incidents.translation: If I talk about placement, the placement records of Manipal are around 99.99 percentage.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor claims a placement rate of 99.99%, which is an inflated and misleading figure that lacks credible evidence, making it a blatant false claim.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor provided misleading placement claims about Manipal Institute of Technology, which could significantly influence the student's decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh 360 seats toh matlab abhi agar main on an average bhi pakad ke chaloon, toh lagbhag 36 se 37,000 bachchon ke hi application accept karegi university starting mein. Aur ho sakta hai abhi jo free of cost aa rahe hain, baad mein fir charge karne lage.
incidents.translation: So, if I consider an average, it means that the university will accept applications from about 36 to 37,000 students at the start. And it is possible that those who are applying free of cost now may start charging later.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency by suggesting that the free application may not last, creating pressure on the student to apply immediately without providing any evidence for this claim.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about potential future charges creates a sense of urgency that is misleading, as it lacks substantiation.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dekho university jo hai aapki woh NAAC A++ grading ki hai.
incidents.translation: Look, the university has a NAAC A++ grading.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents the NAAC A++ grading as a definitive indicator of quality without acknowledging that this grading can be subject to interpretation and may not reflect the actual educational experience.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But iska jo highest package hai woh hai aapka 80 LPA ke something, 80 to 85, aur jo average jaata hai aapka woh around 9 to 11 LPA ke something jaata hai.
incidents.translation: But the highest package is around 80 LPA, 80 to 85, and the average goes around 9 to 11 LPA.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents placement figures that may not be verified or contextualized, potentially misleading the student about the actual placement scenario.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh agar is teeno mein se aapko kisi ek mein bhi admission milta hai na, to uske baad phir aapko package ki tension lene ki zaroorat nahi hai us time pe.
incidents.translation: So if you get admission in any one of these three, then you won't need to worry about the package at that time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a guaranteed outcome regarding admission and future placement success, which is misleading and creates unrealistic expectations.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several misleading claims regarding the university's accreditation and placement statistics, which could sway the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To payment karne se pehle mujhe ek baar connect kar lena aap hai na.
incidents.translation: Before making the payment, make sure to connect with me once.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the student must connect before making a payment, which creates a sense of pressure without clear justification.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aapke paas teen options available ho jayenge.
incidents.translation: You will have three options available.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student will definitely have multiple options available for admission, which could mislead the student into thinking they have guaranteed opportunities.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise on admission opportunities, which could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Uska jo hai na, application fees waise to 1400 hai but aaj ke liye na wo free of cost ho gaya aapka.
incidents.translation: The application fees is normally 1400, but today it has become free of cost for you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free today as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur iska placement jo aapka highest gaya tha wo 50 LPA gaya tha.
incidents.translation: And the highest placement for this was 50 LPA.
incidents.why_misselling: Claiming a highest placement of 50 LPA without verification can mislead the student about the college's actual placement performance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about the application being free and inflated placement figures, which could mislead the student regarding the college's value and urgency to apply.

incidents.counsellor_quote: M.S. Ramaiah gives you a 100% placement guarantee.
incidents.translation: M.S. Ramaiah gives you a 100% placement guarantee.
incidents.why_misselling: Claiming a 100% placement guarantee is a blatant false claim as it cannot be assured for all students regardless of their performance or market conditions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a misleading claim about guaranteed placements, which is a clear case of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi aap waived off, matlab abhi aap apply bhi kar sakte ho, there will be no application fee as of now.
incidents.translation: Currently, your application fee is waived off, which means you can apply without any application fee.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fee as a primary reason to apply, which can be seen as a manipulative nudge rather than focusing on the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aur usko appear karne ka benefit bhi ye hai ki university ke exams ka na jo difficulty level aap check karoge, competition aap check karoge to compare to JEE, compare to any other state level exam thoda sa slightly lower hota hai aur hamare selection ke chances bhi zyada rehte hai.
incidents.translation: The benefit of appearing for the university exams is that the difficulty level is slightly lower compared to JEE and other state-level exams, which increases our chances of selection.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the university's exam is easier than JEE, implying a higher chance of admission without substantiating this claim, which can mislead the student about their actual chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the waived application fee as a primary motivator and made claims about the ease of admission that could mislead the student regarding their chances of acceptance.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to abhi main aapka fir Amity ka complete kar deta hu.
incidents.translation: So now I will complete your application for Amity.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pushing the application for Amity University without addressing the student's concerns about fees and suitability, creating a sense of urgency without valid reasons.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to wo bhi aap at least apply karke rakh do as a backup option.
incidents.translation: So you should at least apply for it as a backup option.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests applying to Amity University without fully disclosing the high fees involved, which could mislead the student about the financial implications.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited signs of pushing the application for Amity University while glossing over important financial details, which could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Hansika aap Amity Gurgaon ke liye bhi apply kar dete because uska bhi jo application hai na wo free hai, uske liye koi charges nahi hai.
incidents.translation: Hansika, you should also apply for Amity Gurgaon because its application is free, there are no charges for it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit for the student's profile or interests.

incidents.counsellor_quote: But jo students hai wo khud unko approach karte hai.
incidents.translation: But the students themselves approach them.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that admission is easily attainable without emphasizing the competitive nature of the process, which could mislead the student about their chances.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on free applications and the ease of admission could mislead the student into thinking that these factors are more significant than they actually are.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To better is this ki hum pehle hi apply kar le.
incidents.translation: It is better that we apply first.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies urgency by suggesting that the application could become paid at any moment, which creates unnecessary pressure to apply immediately without clear evidence of a deadline.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing creates a sense of urgency that may mislead the student into applying without fully considering their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: if you will apply early, there is a higher chances you can get some scholarship also. Because on first come first serve basis, they are providing you scholarship.
incidents.translation: if you apply early, there is a higher chance you can get some scholarship as they provide scholarships on a first come first serve basis.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying early guarantees a scholarship, which can be misleading as it suggests a certainty that may not exist.

incidents.counsellor_quote: So I am talking about the average placement that is 8 lakh 50,000. If you are getting a job after pursuing B.Tech from Alliance, so there is a higher chances you will get placed of a job of 8 lakh 50.
incidents.translation: I am talking about the average placement of 8.5 lakh. If you graduate from Alliance, there is a higher chance you will get a job with that salary.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests a high likelihood of achieving a specific salary post-graduation, which may not accurately reflect the job market realities.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made claims about scholarships and job placements that could mislead the student regarding the certainty of outcomes.

incidents.counsellor_quote: IILM ke liye to aap aaj hi apply karo because yahan pe kya hai na they give you the like admission on the basis of first come first serve.
incidents.translation: You should apply for IILM today because they give you admission on the basis of first come first serve.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pressuring the student to apply immediately by implying that there is a significant advantage to applying today, which may not be substantiated by actual admission policies.

incidents.counsellor_quote: they do offer some scholarship, aap uske liye bhi eligible ho jaoge
incidents.translation: They do offer some scholarships, you will also be eligible for that.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student will be eligible for scholarships without clarifying the conditions or requirements, which can mislead the student into thinking they are guaranteed financial aid.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited pressure tactics and made potentially misleading claims about scholarships, which could influence the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: limited applications ke liye hi inhone extend kiya hai. Aur benefit ye rahega ki jitna jaldi hum fill kar denge to benefit hamara wahi rahega ki hamara profile bhi phase one me consider ho jaaye.
incidents.translation: They have extended it for limited applications. And the benefit will be that the sooner we fill it, the better our profile will be considered in phase one.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a fabricated urgency by suggesting that filling the application quickly is crucial for consideration, without providing concrete evidence of a limited number of applications or a real deadline.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To isiliye aapko pehle se approach karte hai ki aapka application hamare paas submit hai. Aapke ye marks hai to is aapke preferences ye the to hum aapko ye offer kar sakte hai.
incidents.translation: That's why they approach you first because your application is submitted with us. Based on your marks and preferences, we can offer you this.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student's application will be prioritized based on their marks and preferences, which may mislead the student into believing they have a guaranteed chance of admission.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements create a misleading sense of urgency and overpromise the likelihood of admission based on the student's profile, which constitutes clear misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Matlab, waise iska eleven hundred rupees ka application fees rehta hai, gyarah sau ka, but agar hum aaj apply karte hai to there will be no application fees.
incidents.translation: I mean, normally there is an application fee of eleven hundred rupees, but if we apply today, there will be no application fees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is using the waived application fee as a primary reason to encourage the student to apply, which can be seen as a pressure tactic rather than focusing on the college's merits.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan haan haan. So that is for limited student for limited time only.
incidents.translation: Yes yes yes. So that is for limited students for a limited time only.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by stating that the waived application fee is available for a limited time, which can mislead the student into thinking they must act quickly without considering other options.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that create a sense of urgency and emphasized the free application as a primary motivator, which can mislead the student regarding the decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Koi nahi Symbiosis to kar hi lo dekho kyunki entrance de dega baccha na abhi kyunki preparation hai main isliye keh raha hu aapse hai na?
incidents.translation: No worries, just go ahead with Symbiosis because the child will give the entrance now since the preparation is there, that's why I'm saying this to you.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is pushing the student to apply to Symbiosis by implying urgency without providing substantial reasons, which can be seen as manipulative.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi nahi 9,000 to nahi hai.
incidents.translation: No, no, it's not 9,000.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor dismisses the student's concern about the fees without providing clear information, which can mislead the student regarding the actual costs involved.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor exhibited pressure tactics and glossed over important fee information, which could mislead the student regarding the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Amity me to dekho unko mujhe pata hai Amity me 100% ek se do campus me unka secure ho jayega.
incidents.translation: Look, I know that at Amity, 100% will be secured in one or two campuses.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor guarantees admission at Amity University, which is a blatant false claim and misleads the student into believing that admission is assured.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a clear false claim about guaranteed admission at Amity University, which constitutes a case of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Nahi nahi badhega kyunki dekho Amity mein main to be honest aapko bataunga vaise chalta hai process aapka May-June tak bhi chalta hai vaise, theek hai?
incidents.translation: No, it won't increase because see, in Amity, to be honest, I will tell you, the process can go on until May-June, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that the admission process will continue until May-June, which creates a false sense of urgency to apply now, suggesting that seats may not be available later.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Toh abhi matlab kya hai ki aapke paas khaali yeh ho jaana chahiye ki aap offer letter leke rakho apne paas college ka, hai na?
incidents.translation: So, what it means is that you should only have this, that you keep the offer letter of the college with you, right?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that simply having the offer letter guarantees admission, which is misleading as it downplays the competitive nature of college admissions.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding the urgency of the admission process and the assurance of admission based on holding an offer letter.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Ye aapka Mumbai me campus hai. That is also one of the good university jo aapka shortlisting jo karte hai on the basis of MHT CET ya JEE ke basis pe plus aapka jo 12th aur 10th ka score hai uske basis pe bhi consider karte hai profile.
incidents.translation: This is your campus in Mumbai. That is also one of the good universities that shortlist you based on MHT CET or JEE as well as your 12th and 10th scores.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply without providing substantial reasons for the university's fit for the student, which can mislead the student into thinking the application is the most important factor.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To aap apply karke rakh lo. Phir baad me aap decide karna kya offer aapko milta hai university ke end se.
incidents.translation: So you should apply now. Later you can decide what offer you get from the university.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting that the student should apply immediately without allowing time for consideration, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed tactics that emphasized the free application and created a sense of urgency, which can mislead the student into applying without fully considering their options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aap abhi apply karte ho, to iska application ka jo fees hai that is also waived off. To without any charges aap apply bhi isme kar sakte ho.
incidents.translation: if you apply now, the application fee is waived off. So you can apply without any charges.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the waived application fee as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that this is the most important factor rather than the college's fit for their needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar maan lijiye jaise Pune se aapne kiya, lekin agar aapko Bangalore mein job opportunity mil raha hai because Bangalore is also a really growing city, IT ke sector ke maamle mein.
incidents.translation: if you have done it from Pune, but if you are getting job opportunities in Bangalore because Bangalore is also a really growing city in the IT sector.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that job opportunities in Bangalore are a compelling reason to apply, creating a sense of urgency without clear evidence of actual job availability or the student's fit for that location.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used the waived application fee and job opportunities in Bangalore as key selling points, which could mislead the student regarding the importance of these factors in their decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To isme kya hai ki application fees nahi lag rahi hai abhi.
incidents.translation: So what happens is that there are no application fees right now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the absence of application fees as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit or benefits, which can mislead the student into thinking that the lack of fees is the most important factor.

incidents.counsellor_quote: average package iska 20 lakh ka hai. Highest salary 60 lakh gayi hai 2025 mein.
incidents.translation: The average package is around 20 lakhs. The highest salary has gone up to 60 lakhs in 2025.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor presents placement figures without verifying their accuracy, which could mislead the student regarding the college's actual placement performance.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on free application and inflated placement claims for Plaksha could mislead the student into making a decision based on these factors rather than a comprehensive evaluation of the college.

incidents.college_or_course: Chandigarh University, Galgotias College of Engineering, RIT Roorkee
incidents.counsellor_quote: To humne jab initially apply kar diya, jab ye dono cheezein hamare paas hongi aur hum college ko approach karenge ki we want to take admission there, to chances of getting admission bright hamare honge rather than wo baccha jo us time apply kar raha hai.
incidents.translation: So when we initially apply, when we have both these things and we approach the college saying we want to take admission there, our chances of getting admission will be bright compared to the student who applies at that time.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying early guarantees a higher chance of admission, which is misleading as admission is not guaranteed and depends on various factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Last date mein koi issue nahi hai. Hum last date ko approach bhi nahi kar rahe hain, theek hai?
incidents.translation: There is no issue with the last date. We are not even approaching the last date, okay?
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor downplays the importance of application deadlines, which may mislead the student into thinking there is ample time when there may not be.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding the chances of admission based on early application and downplayed the urgency of application deadlines, which could confuse the student about the application process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aapko 100% tak ka full waiver milta hai kyunki aapko LPU NEST dena hota hai.
incidents.translation: You get a 100% full waiver because you have to take the LPU NEST exam.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a guaranteed full scholarship based on exam performance, which can be misleading as it suggests certainty in receiving the waiver without acknowledging the competitive nature of the exam.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Abhi Feb mein iske kuch hi slots bache hue hain.
incidents.translation: Only a few slots are left for February.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting limited availability of slots without providing evidence, which can pressure the student into making a quick decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's claims about guaranteed scholarships and inflated urgency regarding admission slots indicate clear cases of misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: toh easily aap 8 se 10 lakh ka package first year mein hi accept kar sakte ho jo aapko mil sakta mil milega hi milega.
incidents.translation: So you can easily accept a package of 8 to 10 lakhs in the first year, which you will definitely get.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim that the student will definitely receive a package of 8 to 10 lakhs in the first year is misleading, as it guarantees an outcome that cannot be assured.

incidents.counsellor_quote: reason being over the years aap check karoge na VIT ka, to unka highest package 1 CR tak gaya hai aur average 9 se 10 lakh jaati hai.
incidents.translation: The reason being, over the years, if you check VIT, their highest package has gone up to 1 crore and the average is around 9 to 10 lakhs.
incidents.why_misselling: While sharing placement statistics is common, the way it is presented here implies a certainty of similar outcomes for the student, which may not be realistic.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims regarding placement packages at VIT, suggesting guaranteed outcomes that could mislead the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapko jitna jaldi apply karayenge woh hi bachhe ke liye achha hoga kyunki aapko to pata hoga early bird kya hota hai.
incidents.translation: The sooner you apply, the better it will be for the child because you know what early bird means.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency without clear justification, implying that applying quickly is crucial for success, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 28 se pehle aap fill kar dijiye kyunki abhi early bird chal raha hai.
incidents.translation: Please fill it before the 28th because the early bird is currently running.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests a fabricated urgency regarding the application deadline, which may not be substantiated, pressuring the student to act quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar aap abhi nahi karwayenge to jab student ka yeh boards khatam ho jayega na, tab aap bilkul apply nahi kar sakte.
incidents.translation: If you don't apply now, once the student's boards are over, you won't be able to apply at all.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement implies that failing to apply immediately will result in a complete loss of opportunity, which is misleading and creates undue pressure.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor employed pressure tactics and created a false sense of urgency regarding application deadlines, which could mislead the student into making a rushed decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Boards ke baad to late ho jayega bahut.
incidents.translation: After the boards, it will be very late.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that applying after the board exams will result in missing out on opportunities, which may not be true.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Kolkata mein bahut saare colleges hain jinhone resident ke liye apna application free kiya tha. Even bahut saare ka to 28th ko close ho gaya hai.
incidents.translation: There are many colleges in Kolkata that have made their applications free for residents. Even many of them closed on the 28th.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that many applications have already closed, which may pressure the student into making a hasty decision without verifying the actual deadlines.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used pressure tactics to create a sense of urgency regarding college applications, which could mislead the student into feeling rushed to apply.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to aapko main 200 ka cashback main aapko Shiksha ke taraf se main aapko cashback karwa dunga.
incidents.translation: I will give you a cashback of 200 from Shiksha if you proceed with your application through this number.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is using the cashback offer as a primary incentive to push the student to apply, which can be seen as a form of misleading motivation rather than focusing on the college's merits.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's emphasis on the cashback offer could mislead the student into prioritizing financial incentives over the suitability of the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan like for the phase one. Reason being na every phase has their own cut off or every phase they have their own benefit like if we apply in phase one so benefit is the cut off is slightly.
incidents.translation: Yes, like for phase one. The reason being that every phase has its own cutoff or benefits, like if we apply in phase one, the benefit is that the cutoff is slightly better.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that applying in phase one is significantly more beneficial without providing clear evidence, which creates a sense of urgency that may not be justified.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan chances bahut high rahenge, matlab ye maan lo ke mil hi jayega aapko.
incidents.translation: Yes, the chances will be very high, meaning you can assume that you will definitely get it.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that the student will likely get admission based on their application, which can be misleading as it implies a guarantee of admission without acknowledging the competitive nature of the process.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor used inflated urgency and made claims about high chances of admission that could mislead the student into thinking their admission is almost guaranteed.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Dusra agar aap us mindset se kar rahe hain ki ek baar hamara counselling mein hum dekh lete hain then no, aapko ye karna abhi rahega jo best abhi bacche ke liye rehta hai JEE aspirant ke liye kyunki ek smart JEE aspirant na apne saath kam se kam 12 backup leke chalta hai.
incidents.translation: Secondly, if you are thinking that we will see in the counseling later, then no, you need to do this now which is best for the JEE aspirant because a smart JEE aspirant carries at least 12 backups with him.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency by implying that the student must act immediately to secure options, which may not be entirely true and could pressure the student into making hasty decisions.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To ye already late hai.
incidents.translation: So this is already late.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is suggesting that the student is already late in the application process, which may not be accurate and serves to pressure the student into applying quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's framing of urgency and lateness in the application process creates pressure on the student, which can lead to hasty decisions regarding college applications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Iske application bhi free hain aap bacche ka bharwa dijiye koi dikkat nahi hai.
incidents.translation: The applications are also free, you can fill it for the child, there is no problem.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, which downplays the importance of evaluating the college's fit for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's mention of the free application could be seen as a nudge to encourage the student to apply without fully considering other important factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: agar maan lijiye 1000 rank ke under aa rahe hain to aapko chances hote hain ki uske basis pe scholarship bhi milne ka aur main campus milne ka chances hote hain.
incidents.translation: if you fall under the 1000 rank, you have chances of getting a scholarship and also getting the main campus.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a high likelihood of receiving a scholarship and admission to the main campus based solely on the student's rank, which can be misleading as admission is not guaranteed and depends on various factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to agar maan lijiye aapka JEE Mains me cut off kam bhi aa raha hai to bhi chances hote hain ki aapko seats mil jaye university me.
incidents.translation: so if your JEE Mains cut off is low, there are still chances that you will get seats in the university.
incidents.why_misselling: This statement creates a false sense of urgency and assurance about admission despite potentially low scores, which is misleading.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made several misleading claims regarding admission chances and urgency, which could sway the student's decision-making process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aap ye charo jagah pe apply kar sakte ho without paying anything.
incidents.translation: You can apply to all these four places without paying anything.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the applications can be submitted without payment, which may mislead the student into thinking that applying is entirely free without considering other potential costs or obligations.

incidents.counsellor_quote: 28 February tak jo inke applications hain wo jo live hain wo 100% tak ki scholarship ke liye live hain.
incidents.translation: The applications for these are live until February 28 for up to 100% scholarship.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by suggesting a deadline for scholarship applications without providing clear evidence of the scholarship availability, which could pressure the student to apply quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statements regarding free applications and urgency around scholarship deadlines could mislead the student into making hasty decisions without fully understanding the implications.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aapka Alliance pe 200 ka main cashback provide kara deti hoon Shiksha ki taraf se.
incidents.translation: I will provide you a cashback of 200 from Shiksha for Alliance.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is using the promise of cashback as a primary incentive to encourage the user to share their UPI ID, which can be seen as a manipulative tactic rather than focusing on the actual merits of the college or course.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on cashback as an incentive raises concerns about the integrity of the counselling process.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Haan. Toh ab Sharda ka phir fill kar lo aaj.
incidents.translation: Yes. So now fill the Sharda form today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free of cost as a primary reason to apply, which can mislead the student into thinking that the application is the most important aspect rather than the suitability of the college.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Phir wahi hai na ki jaise cashback ke liye maine convince kiya tha ki aaj tak ka ho jayega.
incidents.translation: Then it's the same that I convinced you for the cashback that it has to be done by today.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor creates a sense of urgency by implying that the cashback opportunity is time-sensitive, which may not be true, pressuring the student to act quickly.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's approach included misleading urgency and a focus on the free application, which could pressure the student into making a hasty decision without considering other options.

incidents.counsellor_quote: unka batch placement rate bhi 100% jaata hai.
incidents.translation: Their batch placement rate also goes up to 100%.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor's claim of a 100% placement rate is misleading without evidence, as such guarantees are typically unverifiable and can create unrealistic expectations for the student.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made a misleading claim about the placement rate of K.L.E. Technological University, which could unduly influence the student's decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: in fact, kaafi college campuses hain Amity ke jo abhi current mein free applications provide kar rahe hain.
incidents.translation: In fact, many college campuses of Amity are currently providing free applications.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes the free application as a primary reason to apply, which could mislead the student into thinking that the free application is the most important factor rather than the suitability of the college.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application process for Amity University could mislead the student about the importance of fit and suitability for their needs.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Like early birds benefits. So what is called early bird benefit is it is for first 500 applications, they are offering extra scholarship chances and the highest chance of getting seats in the university.
incidents.translation: Like early birds benefits. So what is called early bird benefit is it is for first 500 applications, they are offering extra scholarship chances and the highest chance of getting seats in the university.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies that there are limited benefits for the first 500 applications, which creates a sense of urgency that may not be substantiated, potentially misleading the student into applying quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's mention of early bird benefits creates an inflated sense of urgency that could pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

incidents.counsellor_quote: aap mujhe yeh bataiye aap abhi apply kar chuke hain ya nahi kiya hai? Kyunki iske slots zyada nahi hain.
incidents.translation: Please tell me if you have already applied or not? Because there are not many slots left.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor implies a sense of urgency by suggesting that slots are limited without providing any evidence or specific details, which can pressure the student into making a hasty decision.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor created a sense of urgency regarding the application slots for VIT Vellore, which could mislead the student into feeling pressured to apply quickly.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To main ye bolungi ki first aap Amity se apply kar lo kyunki Amity ke application form me abhi koi fees nahi deni hai.
incidents.translation: So I would say that first you should apply to Amity because there is no fee to be paid for the Amity application form right now.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the free application as a primary reason to apply to Amity, rather than discussing the college's fit for the student's profile or interests.

incidents.counsellor_quote: Aap sirf ek form fill kar lo, baaki fir main aapka update kar dungi Bombay, Pune ka.
incidents.translation: Just fill one form, and then I will update you about Bombay and Pune.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is creating a sense of urgency to fill out the application without providing sufficient information about the colleges, which could mislead the student into thinking they need to act quickly without proper consideration.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application and urgency to apply without discussing the student's specific needs raises concerns about potential misselling.

incidents.counsellor_quote: To iske liye agar aap fill up karna chahte ho to mere paas coupon code available hai, koi fees nahi lagegi isme.
incidents.translation: So for this, if you want to fill up, I have a coupon code available, there will be no fees for this.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor is emphasizing the absence of fees as a primary reason to apply, rather than discussing the college's fit for the student.

incidents.counsellor_quote: to agar bacche ka accha score aata hai to automatic bacche ko admission mil jayega.
incidents.translation: So if the child scores well, then the child will automatically get admission.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor suggests that good scores will guarantee admission, which is misleading as admission is not solely based on scores.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor made misleading claims about the application process and admission guarantees, which could mislead the student regarding their chances and the application requirements.

incidents.counsellor_quote: is ke baare mein dekh lete hain. Ye sir Bangalore ka hi college hai, right? Iska jo hai yahan par. Isme bhi accept karta hai KCET ka. KCET ka exam accept karta hai ye, iski jo fees hai wo main aapko bata deti hu. Ten lakh rupees fees hai iska.
incidents.translation: Let's take a look at this. This is a college in Bangalore, right? It accepts KCET. The fees for this is ten lakh rupees.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that the application is free without providing substantial reasons for the college's fit for the student, which can mislead the student into thinking that the college is a good option solely based on the free application.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's focus on the free application may mislead the student regarding the college's suitability without discussing other important factors.

incidents.counsellor_quote: KIIT mein koi fees nahi pay karni hai aapko.
incidents.translation: You don't have to pay any fees for KIIT.
incidents.why_misselling: The counsellor emphasizes that there are no fees for KIIT as a primary reason to encourage the student to complete the application, which may mislead the student into thinking that the application is entirely free of costs without discussing other potential fees or expenses.

misselling.overall_notes: The counsellor's statement about KIIT not requiring any fees could mislead the student regarding the overall costs associated with the college application process.
